chapters.title: Inside The Great Deku Tree
chapters.intro: The Great Deku Tree tells of the forest children, the Kokiri, and of the lone boy in the village who has no fairy — then we see that boy's nightmare: a lowered drawbridge, a white horse galloping out carrying a cowled woman and a sad little girl, and an evil-looking man on a black stallion raising his hand toward Link. The Deku Tree summons Navi to find the boy who will change the destiny of Hyrule; after a flight over the village she finds our hero's house and yells at him until he wakes.

steps.detail: Once you gain control, walk outside and you'll meet your childhood friend Saria. Climb down and talk to her if you wish — she expresses her excitement over your new fairy and urges you on to see the Great Deku Tree.

keywords: Get a Sword and Shield to pass Mido
steps.detail: The path to the Great Deku Tree is blocked by Mido, who won't let you past until you get a Sword and Shield. The shield costs 40 Rupees at the shop, so get the sword first — it makes the rupee gathering easier.

keywords: Crawl into the Forest Training Center hole
steps.detail: The fenced Forest Training Center is in the southwestern area of the village (bottom left on your map). Crawl through the small hole in the wall next to the sign. Inside, round the corner for a Blue Rupee (5), wait for the giant boulder to pass, follow it and take the first right for another Blue Rupee (5). The next alcove holds a giant chest with the Kokiri Sword.
Tap the gear button on the bottom screen (3DS) or pause and open the Equipment menu (N64), and equip the Kokiri Sword. Work your way back to the hole and crawl back through.

keywords: Buy the Deku Shield at the shop
steps.detail: The village is full of little secret rupee spots, and many can be collected repeatedly by entering a building and coming back out. Once you have 40 Rupees, head to the Shop (the northeast building) — the girl sitting above the doorway shows you can Z-target and talk to her — and purchase the Deku Shield.
Make sure both the sword and shield are equipped before speaking with Mido. After downright insulting you, he reluctantly lets you pass.
Near the end of the path, three Withered Deku Babas pop up — the first enemies of the game. They don't move or attack; slash them and collect the Deku Sticks they drop.

keywords: Talk to the Great Deku Tree
steps.detail: Talk to the Great Deku Tree. He explains the nightmares come from the evil descending upon Hyrule, that he has been cursed, and that you can break the curse if you're willing. Say yes and enter his mouth.

Main Room — you immediately meet the regular Deku Baba, a good opportunity to practice targeting + shield play; you'll probably pick up a few Deku Nuts. Equip both Deku Sticks and Deku Nuts. If you got hurt, slash the nearby weeds (they grow back) for a chance at Recovery Hearts.

Note the web on the floor — nothing to do with it yet. Ascend the wooden tower by the ladder or the climbable vines, then work clockwise around the upper area. Past some vines with spiders on them, open the large chest for the Dungeon Map (each dungeon has one). Continue right, jump across to the last platform and go through the door.

First Deku Scrub Room — stay back so it doesn't burrow, target it and reflect its shot with your shield, then hunt it down before it gets back in its nest. It gives a clue: when you fall really far, hold the stick in the direction you're facing to roll. It leaves Recovery Hearts behind and the next door opens.

Slingshot Room — jump on the crumbling platform and across to the next (or climb the vines if you fall). The large chest holds the dungeon's treasure: the Fairy Slingshot. It uses Deku Seeds for ammo, which you can now find by slashing bushes.

keywords: Shoot the ladder above the door
steps.detail: If you need hearts, climb the nearby vines to a hidden alcove with a small chest (Recovery Heart). The platform you came in on broke, so target the suspicious ladder directly above the entry door and shoot it with the Slingshot to drop it into place. Head back to the main room.

keywords: Climb the vines to the top
steps.detail: Back near the Dungeon Map chest, shoot the Skullwalltulas on the vines first — otherwise they'll knock you off while climbing. Climb to the very top, shimmy left or right, and drop onto the platform at the highest level of the room.

Follow the path and watch the shadows — a Big Skulltula drops down. You can only hit its backside: stand close enough to bring it down, wait for it to show its vulnerable side, then strike. Go through the door up here.

Platform/Compass Room — step on the switch to raise the three floor platforms temporarily, jump along them to the far side and open the large chest for the Compass (one per dungeon — shows your position, chests, elevators and the boss on the map).

Jump down and kill the Big Skulltula on the right, then the Deku Baba in front of the vines. Climb back up, step on the switch again and platform to the LEFT alcove this time. The small chest holds a Recovery Heart, but directly behind it is a Gold Skulltula — kill it and touch the token. There are 100 of these spiders throughout Hyrule; collecting tokens earns prizes in one of the villages.

keywords: Light the second torch with a Deku Stick
steps.detail: The door you came from is barred. Of the two torches here one is unlit: light a Deku Stick on the lit torch and carry the flame to the other. Once the door opens, slash your sword to put the stick away and conserve it.

keywords: Leap from the top floor onto the web
steps.detail: Back on the top floor, kill one of the Big Skulltulas, then look over the edge: the floor web is directly below. Leap off the top floor and fall onto the CENTER of the web to break it — hitting the edge just bounces you like a trampoline (climb back up and retry). You fall into a cavern partially filled with water.

Main Room (Low) — climb the nearby platform and step on the switch to light a torch, burning the cobwebs that covered it. Light a Deku Stick, jump to the shallow water and run to the opposite platform: if you're fast enough you can burn the cobwebs covering the far door.

keywords: Beat the Deku Scrub for a clue
steps.detail: Second Deku Scrub Room — reflect its attack with your shield like before and chase it down; it gives another hint that matters later in the dungeon. Slash the bushes for Deku Seeds if you're low, then shoot the silver eye switch above the next door with the Slingshot and go through.

keywords: Ride the platform under the log
steps.detail: Spiked Log & Water Room — jump in and swim to the left side where an underwater switch lowers the water temporarily. Quickly swim back, climb the ramp, wait for the floating platform to come to your side, jump on and ride under the spiked log.

keywords: Push the block under the doorway
steps.detail: On the other side, kill the Big Skulltula, then push/pull the stone block until it sits under the doorway. Walk into the block and press the button to climb it, then enter the door.
Torch Room — kill the Deku Baba and Withered Deku Baba and collect their drops. Two torches are unlit and one is lit; carry the flame with a Deku Stick. Once both are lit, go through the open door.

keywords: Snipe the Big Skulltula from the door
steps.detail: Larvae Room — from the doorway, target the Big Skulltula straight ahead (its back is to you) and kill it with two Slingshot shots before entering. Inside, avoid the three shadows in the center: stepping near them drops eggs that hatch into Gohma Larvae. They're only a pain in groups — target one, get close and slash.

Two cobwebs here, but only the far one leads onward for now. Light a Deku Stick on the nearby torch, run it to the farthest cobweb, burn it, slash to conserve the stick and crawl through the hole. NOTE: the other cobweb leads to a walled-off room holding a Gold Skulltula we can't reach yet — we'll return with the appropriate items.

keywords: Push the block off the edge
steps.detail: You're now on the upper level of the lowest floor. Kill the Deku Babas if you like, then push the nearby stone block off the edge for a shortcut. The last cobweb in the floor must be burned: the two torches up here are too high, so jump down by your new block shortcut, light a Deku Stick at the low torch used earlier, climb back up quickly and burn the cobweb (roll into it or swipe the stick). The deepest part of the dungeon opens below.

keywords: Stun the Deku Scrubs in order: 2, 3, 1
steps.detail: In the final area you can dive for Recovery Hearts if needed. On land, reflect the Deku Nuts back at the three Deku Scrubs — stun them in the wrong order and you start over. The clue from earlier gives the order: 2, 3, 1 — middle first, then right, then left.
Catch the last Scrub before it flees — it gives a clue about the boss in the next room. Grab hearts if you need them, then enter the boss door.

keywords: Stun her when her eye is red
steps.detail: Look up at the queen on the ceiling to begin the battle. Her attacks: wobbling toward you to fall on top of you (shield it or step aside), and climbing to the ceiling to lay three eggs that hatch into Gohma Larvae (slash each egg before it hatches, or two sword swipes / a Jump Attack each). To harm her, hit her with Deku Nuts or the Slingshot while her eye is red (right before or after either attack) — the Slingshot is by far the most effective — then smack her with the sword while she's down. Easiest: wait for her to climb to the ceiling and stun her then (a far longer stun); done right, she falls in under a minute.

missable.deadline: During this fight — Spider Net requires defeating Queen Gohma before she can retreat to the ceiling

steps.detail: After the scene of her disintegrating, snag the Heart Container left in her remains, then step into the shiny light to teleport out of the dungeon.

Back outside, the Deku Tree reveals what happened: an evil man from the desert demanded one of the three Spiritual Stones which together open a door to the Sacred Realm, where the Triforce — raw power left by the three Goddesses who created Hyrule — waits. Refused, the man cursed the tree. The Deku Tree urges you to seek out "the Princess of Destiny" in Hyrule Castle, gives you the Spiritual Stone of the Forest he was after… and then dies.

Back in the village, Mido stops you and jumps to the conclusion that YOU killed the Great Deku Tree, then leaves before you can get a word in. Nothing to do here for the moment — leave via the exit on the opposite side of the village.

keywords: Receive the Fairy Ocarina from Saria
steps.detail: On the bridge to Hyrule Field, Saria pops out to say goodbye. As a parting gift she gives you the Fairy Ocarina, a mystical instrument for playing magic songs throughout your journey.

Exiting gives an overview of the field — the castle on the far side is the goal. Around the corner the owl Kaepora Gaebora interrupts: he reaffirms you must go to Hyrule Castle and explains the map. When he's done, select the BOTTOM option (No, I DON'T want to hear it again!) — the game swaps his responses to trip you up throughout.

keywords: Run straight to the castle before nightfall
steps.detail: Run straight toward the castle using the mini-map: quick and direct, you'll arrive before nightfall; dilly-dally and the drawbridge rises, locking you out. Off the path at night you'll meet Stalchildren (they don't appear if you wait on the road); at sunrise the drawbridge lowers. NOTE: a LOT is already accessible now (Lost Woods, Gerudo Valley, Lake Hylia, Lon Lon Ranch, Kakariko Village hold heart pieces, gold skulltulas and other items), but the storyline objectives there are still blocked — to optimize the path, this guide collects those goodies in the upcoming chapters.
Head into the heart of the Market. Nothing here is required right now — the next three steps are optional goodies; skip ahead to Hyrule Castle to continue the main quest.

Immediately after the drawbridge, enter the first door — the guardhouse. The bored soldier lets you tear the place to shreds: great early Rupees, and one of the crates in the back of the room next to the guard contains a Gold Skulltula.

You can climb the drawbridge chains just outside town: from the very top of a chain, jump toward the center of the bridge for a Red Rupee — doable on both sides (40 Rupees). A third hidden Red Rupee sits at the bridge's center: roll and then jump off the chains, standing a little away from the wall's edge — trickier, but the first 40 come easy.

The Shooting Gallery (bullseye above the doorway) costs at least 20 Rupees — stock up first. Shoot all the Rupees that pop out; the pattern is the same every time, and Deku Seeds take a moment to travel for the moving ones. Near-misses earn a free replay; only a perfect score wins the prize, and only the FIRST prize matters: a Deku Seed Bullet Bag holding up to 40 seeds for your Slingshot.

keywords: Follow the road toward the castle
steps.detail: Beyond the Market, follow the road toward Hyrule Castle. Kaepora Gaebora appears again: in towns and houses time stands still, while outside (here or Hyrule Field) it flows — to wait for day or night, leave town.
In the area leading to the castle there is a tree on the left. Roll into it and the Gold Skulltula falls out.

keywords: Return to the Market and back
Get the Weird Egg from Malon
steps.detail: The area is littered with guards, and before sneaking past them an item is needed: return to the Market (someone who was there before is now missing), then head back toward the castle. By the vines you now find the red-haired girl, Malon, of Lon Lon Ranch in the center of Hyrule Field. Her father Talon went to deliver Lon Lon Milk to the castle and probably fell asleep — she asks you to wake him if you find him, and gives you… an egg.

Climb the vines and follow the cliff to the end, then jump off on the far side of the gate (away from the guard). Facing the castle: two guards watch the right-hand path, so climb up between the two guards on the right and the single guard up top on the left — easy to pass unnoticed. Stay left (off the bright road) to reach another gate with two guards; on the left, suspicious vines (stones on N64) stick out of the cliff. Climb up and jump down the other side.
Two guards still watch this last gate, so jump into the moat instead and swim along, collecting Rupees on the way. At the far corner, climb out and continue until you find Talon sleeping away.

By now the Weird Egg has likely hatched into a Cucco (if not, wait around). Use it from a C button while standing next to Talon to wake him. He confirms Malon's story — then, on learning she sent you, runs away screaming in fear of his own daughter.

keywords: Stack the crates in the water
steps.detail: Push and pull the two crates into the water — the second snaps on top of the first, giving the height needed to jump to the water flow coming from the castle. Crawl through the hole into the inner walls. NOTE: this must be done during the daytime; at night two guards catch you immediately.

At the courtyard entrance a guard stands on the left. For Money Solves Everything: talk to him and get caught (you're thrown out), then come back — now you can talk to him again, pay a small fee, and he opens the gate. The garden beyond is littered with patrolling guards; pass without getting caught by standing behind them (never to either side) and moving when they're on the far side of a fountain, statue or topiary, or walking away. Past the final obstacle, turn left into the quiet part of the gardens, where there are no guards at all.

missable.deadline: At the Hyrule Courtyard entrance as child — Money Solves Everything requires bribing the gate guard here; once you've entered the courtyard the dialogue can't be triggered again

keywords: Optional: Slingshot the left window (anger a guard)
steps.detail: Right before speaking with Zelda, for Troublemaker: shoot the window to your left with the Slingshot to trigger a short guard dialogue. Then check the cameo pictures in the window on the right if you like, and speak with the princess for perhaps the lengthiest conversation in Zelda history to this point. She reveals the royal family's secret: the Triforce, the three magical triangles the goddesses left behind, grants any wish to whoever gains it — enough to change Hyrule's fate for a hundred years.

missable.deadline: Before talking to Zelda as child — Troublemaker requires Slingshotting the left window here to anger a guard; you can't return to this area after collecting all 3 Spiritual Stones

steps.detail: After a peep through the window at the creepy Gerudo man she was spying on, Zelda recruits you to retrieve the remaining Spiritual Stones and reach the Sacred Realm before Ganondorf does. She gives you a letter carrying her royal authority.

At the exit you meet Zelda's nursemaid Impa, a ninja of sorts loyal to the royal family who believes the young lady's visions. She teaches you your first song: Zelda's Lullaby — then leads you outside the castle and points you toward Kakariko Village. A few other things need doing before actually scaling Death Mountain…

chapters.title: The Mighty Collection
chapters.intro: We have access to a significant portion of the world now, and while there is a TON to do in this chapter, almost nothing in it is essential to the quest — heart pieces, skulltulas and extra items, none required to beat the game. Skip ahead to the next chapter if you only want the main quest.

steps.detail: The next dungeon is up on Death Mountain, but a few things are needed first. Cross Hyrule Field to Kakariko Village, as Impa told you. There's tons to do in this town and around it, much of it time-of-day dependent — for now continue through to the Graveyard via the exit on the far right side of the village.

keywords: Play Zelda's Lullaby on the Triforce symbol
steps.detail: The Graveyard is rainy and mysterious by day (a kid slowly imitates Dampé the gravekeeper); at night, Poes appear — easily beaten, easier avoided. At the far side stand three large graves: reading the two on either side summons a deceased Composer Brother (they look a bit like Mario and Luigi), who reveals they composed a mystical song that moves the sun. Stand on the Triforce symbol before the largest grave and play Zelda's Lullaby — lightning blasts the tombstone open. Hop down the hole.

keywords: Kill the 5 Keese with the Slingshot
steps.detail: Royal Family's Tomb — head down the ramp to meet Keese, the bat-like enemies. Kill all five in this room with your Slingshot to open the opposing door, then climb the massive steps and go through.
The next room holds ReDeads, which freeze you with their gaze and are only safely killed from behind — for now just avoid them. Work around the poisonous pools to the other side of the room.

In the final passage, press A to check the large stone. Navi reads the poetic inscription and you learn the Sun's Song — perhaps one of the handiest songs in the game. Work your way out, using the Sun's Song to stun the ReDeads, and step on the colorful pad in the first room to return to the surface.

keywords: Pull the 4th grave in the back row
steps.detail: Between the last row of graves and the Royal Family's Tomb, face the entrance and pull back the fourth grave from the left to reveal a hole. Inside, play the Sun's Song to stun the ReDead, slash it from behind, then stand on the platform steps and play the Sun's Song again — a large chest appears with our first Piece of a Heart [#1]!

In the first row of graves (toward the area entrance), one grave has three flowers in front of it. Pull it back and jump in: a lone small chest holds the Hylian Shield. We'll need one for the next dungeon — the Market sells it discounted at 50 Rupees, but this one's free!

missable.deadline: Before buying one at the Market — Shield Raider requires obtaining the Hylian Shield for free instead of buying it

steps.detail: Dampé's mini-game runs only between 18:00 and 21:00 (ask the guard at Kakariko's entrance for the time; conveniently it turns night right after getting the Sun's Song). Pay Dampé 10 Rupees per dig at the soft-soil spots — prize locations are random each visit, with a 10% Heart Piece chance per dig, but the heart is guaranteed within the first 15 soil spots he digs. He regularly digs up Rupees too; if you're short on money now, come back later — your wallet fills up fast in this game. The prize to hold out for is Piece of Heart [#2].

Near the graveyard entrance, the Cucco Lady stands before a small fenced pen and offers a reward for putting her chickens back in it. They're scattered across the village, some nearly impossible to reach. Try not to harm them with your weapons — hurt them too much and they sound a war cry and rain doom on you until you leave the area or die. Seriously.

The five reachable cuccos: (1) between the Cucco Lady and the Graveyard entrance; (2) on the ledge next to the Skulltula House in the village center — float over with another chicken from the unfinished building's higher ledge, or knock it down with the Slingshot or a Deku Nut and chase it; (3) at the village entrance; (4) inside the crate near the stairs to the upper area — roll into it to reveal the cucco spy; (5) right in front of the Death Mountain gate guard, near the ramp.

keywords: Toss the cuccos over the fence
steps.detail: The last two cuccos are in the northeast corner, reachable only by near-cheating, two ways: grab another cucco, climb the platform near the windmill entrance and jump off at an angle to land on the skinny fence on the LEFT (closest to the potion shop); or get on the lookout tower, hold Z facing the village center and side-jump left onto a fence, then drop into the secret area. (You could even grab a Piece of Heart early from the guy on the roof here — the walkthrough saves it for later.) Toss each cucco you find over the fence to retrieve afterwards.
(6) is in the lower part of the secret area near a hole — careful not to fall in; throw it over the fence. (7) is up the ladder: grab the unsuspecting cucco and glide back toward the lady with it.
With all seven gathered and in the pen, speak with the Cucco Lady to receive an Empty Bottle! She explains she couldn't do it herself — she's allergic to her own chickens.

keywords: Drop them on the graveyard soft soil
steps.detail: Time for Gold Skulltulas (worth doing at least up to 10 for the wallet upgrade). With the empty bottle, lift the lone rock at the bushes by the Graveyard entrance — bugs scurry out; swipe one with the bottle. Take it to the left side, by the five graves that stand alone, where there's a patch of soft soil: drop the bugs on it and a Gold Skulltula pops out. Collect the token and return to the village.

Back in Kakariko, play the Sun's Song to make it night: five Gold Skulltulas appear around the village — follow the sound. Per the ZD skulltula table: on the building under construction at the village center; at the side of the Skulltula House; in the tree straight ahead of the village entrance (roll into it); near the top of the watch tower (shoot it down with the Slingshot); and on the side of the house closest to the Death Mountain gate.

Enter the House of Skulltula, right across from the construction area. The cursed spider-person in the center explains the family's plight: the Curse of the Spider lifts progressively as you collect Skulltula Tokens — one family member freed at 10, 20, 30, 40, 50 and 100 tokens.

If you've followed the walkthrough, you have at least ten Gold Skulltulas — speak with the kid on the right doing jumping-jacks to receive the Adult Wallet. About the prizes: the first few matter, but only up through the 50-token prize is really worth it (the final one is useless) — collect as many as possible in the first half of the game to bank the good prizes early; past 50 is purely for 100% completion.

keywords: Meet Talon inside the left door
steps.detail: Entirely optional, but there are cool goodies here: head to Lon Lon Ranch in the center of Hyrule Field (use the mini-map). Play the Sun's Song for daytime if needed, then enter the door on your left to meet Talon again — he has a little game prepared involving cuccos. Bring some Rupees; it may take a few tries.

keywords: Tip: toss the strays aside first
steps.detail: Mini-Game: Super Cuccos! Talon shoos the chickens into the other three corners of the room; search each area and toss wrong chickens in a safe direction so you don't grab them twice. Found one? Move to the next corner — easily done within the time limit. To make it immensely easier, toss all the stray cuccos to the base of the stairs BEFORE talking to Talon, leaving only the right ones to pick up. Practically cheating — he's too sleepy to notice.
Catch all three Super Cuccos and Talon insinuates you might wed his daughter, then gifts you the second bottle, filled with Lon Lon Milk.

keywords: Optional: Gold Skulltula in the lone tree
steps.detail: Exit the building and round the corner on the left: roll into the single lonesome tree to make a Gold Skulltula [#6] fall down.

In the center of the corral, Malon chats about the castle and her father, then introduces you to the horse — who immediately runs from you. Keep speaking with her until she asks about "the song her mother wrote", then pull out your Ocarina in front of her: she teaches you Epona's Song.

keywords: Play Epona's Song to a cow for free milk
steps.detail: Epona's Song played in front of cows gets you free Lon Lon Milk — a sweet deal for a while, until you have too many hearts. Otherwise it just makes Epona follow you within the corral for now.

keywords: Optional: night — Gold Skulltula on the feeding trough
steps.detail: Play the Sun's Song to make it night, get outside the corral and follow it clockwise, avoiding the Guays (crows). At the feeding trough — the single wooden overhang in the corral's lower-right corner on your map — sits a Gold Skulltula [#7], here only at night.

keywords: Optional: Heart Piece in the feed silo
steps.detail: Head to the feed silo (the big tower in the bottom-left corner of the map). Inside, push, pull and climb over the crates to reach the far corner, where a small crawl hole leads to our next Piece of a Heart [#3]. That's it for Lon Lon Ranch.

keywords: Bottle bugs from the rock trio
steps.detail: Next destination: Gerudo Valley at the west end of Hyrule Field (the pathway is directly west of Lon Lon Ranch). Just inside, immediately to the right, lift the trio of rocks — under one are bugs; snag some with your bottle, we'll need them shortly.

keywords: Bugs on the soft soil below
steps.detail: Walk forward and pick up the Cucco, then jump off the RIGHT side of the bridge toward the waterfall — there's a landing spot on the left as you look down. The water flows slowly here, so you can swim against the current if you fall in. Nearby, a Gerudo woman in white stands with a cow; right next to her is a soft soil spot — drop the bottled bugs to pop out a Gold Skulltula.

keywords: Optional: Heart Piece behind the waterfall
steps.detail: Drop into the water behind the waterfall to find a hidden hole in the wall with a ladder nearby. Climb it — at the top waits a Piece of a Heart [#16].

keywords: Optional: Heart Piece in the crate
steps.detail: One more Heart Piece here, but you must get back to the top first — and the only way is on foot: jump in the river, let it carry you to Lake Hylia, exit through the north side of that map back to Hyrule Field and return to Gerudo Valley. Now pick up the Cucco and glide off the LEFT side of the bridge to the platform across the way with a lonely crate on it. Roll into the crate for Heart Piece [#17].

keywords: Ride the river to Lake Hylia
Make up the Scarecrow's Song (8 notes)
steps.detail: Jump into the water at Gerudo Valley and the flow carries you to Lake Hylia. At the small fences on the northeast side stand two scarecrows: Pierre (top) and Bonooru (bottom). Speak with Bonooru — he never forgets a tune — and whip out your Ocarina: make up a song 8 notes long. It does nothing yet, but make it something you can REMEMBER; playing those eight notes later performs the Scarecrow's Song, and it'll come in handy.

keywords: Optional: bugs on the Lakeside Lab soft soil
steps.detail: Slash the lone bush on the top ledge by Pierre to find bugs and bottle them, then head to the Lakeside Laboratory (the big house with the chimney) on the opposite side. Drop the bugs on the soft soil there to pop out a Gold Skulltula.

keywords: Optional: night — Gold Skulltula on the pillar island
steps.detail: Swim to the smaller of the two lake islands, in the southeast (the one with the two pillars). Play the Sun's Song to make it night and kill the Gold Skulltula that appears.

Swim to the Fishing Pond in the corner of the area near land. Pay the man 20 Rupees for a pole and fish as long as you like. Basics: A casts and reels, B wiggles, and the stick pulls the line; target a fish, cast (adjust in midair with A if it flies too far), let the lure get close and attract the fish with B + stick; if the wrong one bites, do nothing and it lets go. DON'T SCARE THE FISH or they bolt to deep water. All fish start at fixed spots — the biggest lurks by the arched log near the three sticks in the water; stand on the log itself so there's less line to reel.

The goal is beating the pond's current record. If the controls fight you, just hook a big fish, then hold down + A and Link slowly reels it in — keep holding and odds are he lands it. If it escapes, leave the Pond, return and retry. Any fish over ten pounds nets you a Heart Piece. (ZD has a dedicated Fishing Pond Guide for the deep end.)

keywords: Night: Gold Skulltula behind the Know-It-All House
steps.detail: Head back across Hyrule Field to the center-right of the map — dodge the Peahat on the way (never required to fight, though you could win). In Kokiri Forest, play the Sun's Song for night and go to the fenced area where you got the sword: the lone building up there (the Know-It-All Brothers' House) has a Gold Skulltula on its backside. Wind around, kill it, and grab the token with a targeted Jump Attack.

keywords: Enter the Lost Woods (log above Mido's house)
steps.detail: From the area entrance, head left and climb the little ledge behind Mido's House, climb the vines and go around to the hollowed log that leads into the Lost Woods.

The Lost Woods is a maze that kicks you out on a wrong turn, but there are goodies on the way. Take the right passage first, into a big area with a single tree: shoot the hanging "target" on the lone branch. Hit the middle three times in a row (100 points each) and a Deku Scrub appears, gifting a Deku Seeds Bullet Bag that increases your Slingshot ammunition.

keywords: Optional: win the Imps' Simon game 3×
steps.detail: Jump down and stand on the stump to the right: two flute-playing Imps appear on the nearby stumps. Whip out the Ocarina to join their game — a "Simon": repeat the pattern they play, one added note at a time. Complete three songs in a row without a mistake and the first-time prize is a Piece of a Heart [#3]!

keywords: Follow the music through the maze
Right, left, right, left, forward, left, right
steps.detail: Continue through the Lost Woods by following the music — stand close to each exit and listen for where it's loudest (Kaepora Gaebora pops up after two screens to hint the same). From the start, the correct path is: Right, left, right, left, forward, left, right.

Head forward and a Wolfos appears — you'll meet many of these. Target it, keep your shield up to close in, then stab right after it attacks or when its back is turned. The bars blocking the rest of the area disappear when it falls.

keywords: Cross the maze past the Mad Scrubs
steps.detail: Around the corner lurk Mad Scrubs, a Deku Scrub variant that spits three seeds at a time: Z-target and approach with shield raised, then use Deku Nuts or the sword up close (sniping from afar with the Slingshot is harder). Cross the hedge maze with the mini-map — several dead ends, no real trouble — to the ladder and stairs on the far side. Take the steps to the enormous archway, defeat the two Mad Scrubs guarding it quickly, and walk the final distance into the most sacred part of the Sacred Forest Meadow.

Link and Saria are reunited — she's been the one playing that song you hear in the woods all along, and she feels this place will be important to you both later. She teaches you her song, the same one as the woods.

On the way back, climb the ladder at the bottom of the temple-like ramp to walk along the tops of the grassy walls, skipping all the Mad Scrubs. While up there, note the ladder in the middle leading down to a hole: inside is a fairy fountain — perhaps the easiest to reach in the game, a good future stock-up spot. Kaepora Gaebora appears once more with genuinely useful advice: if you're ever stuck, pull out the Ocarina at suspicious spots — if a musical staff appears, a song goes there. Then get lost on purpose to return to Kokiri.

keywords: Optional: play Saria's Song to the Skull Kid
steps.detail: Re-enter the Lost Woods and take a left to find another Skull Kid playing a flute. He runs if you get close, but stand on the stump and he watches expectantly: whip out the Ocarina and play whatever you like — any learned song makes him happy, but Saria's Song earns our next Piece of a Heart!

keywords: Optional: buy the Deku Stick upgrade from the Business Scrub
steps.detail: Head left once more, to the final area below the bridge; jump into the lowest part and cross to the other side. A Business Scrub attacks — target it and reflect its Deku Nut back to beat it, after which it sells you an upgrade: one of the game's two Deku Stick capacity upgrades (upgrades can be gotten in either order). Do it, do it now!

keywords: Optional: 3 soft-soil Gold Skulltulas (Lost Woods & Kokiri)
steps.detail: Three more Gold Skulltulas wait at soft soil spots in the Lost Woods and Kokiri Forest — bugs are found all over the Lost Woods. Per the ZD table: from the Lost Woods entrance go left, left (soft soil); from the entrance go right, left, right, left, left to a meadow (soft soil ahead); and right behind the Kokiri Shop (soft soil).

keywords: Optional: night — bring the white puppy home
steps.detail: Back at the Market, play the Sun's Song for night: dogs run all over town (don't touch the others — they follow you until you leave). By the blue street-vendor stand near the Bazaar (under the man in the window above), touch the white puppy, then take either right-side exit into the Back Alleys. On the left side, one door is open only at night: the woman in blue inside is worried for her little dog, and if it followed you she's thrilled you brought "Little Richard" back — reward: a Piece of a Heart.

keywords: 20 tokens: claim the Shard of Agony
steps.detail: Return to Kakariko Village — with everything so far you should have 20 Gold Skulltula tokens. At the Skulltula House another family member is freed: speak with him for the Shard of Agony (Stone of Agony on N64), which chimes near secrets (on N64 the controller shakes with a Rumble Pak). Not of great use yet, but handy once we have some additional items.

keywords: Show Zelda's Letter to the Death Mountain guard
steps.detail: Climb to the top of Kakariko Village and show Zelda's Letter to the Death Mountain guard. He laughs at you, opens the gate, tells you to get a shield, and mentions his kid desperately wants a mask from the Happy Mask Shop in Hyrule Town (a side quest for a later chapter). If you don't have the Hylian Shield yet: the Market Bazaar now sells it for 60 Rupees instead of 80 since the guard put in a word — or it's free in the Kakariko Graveyard, covered last chapter. Head through the gate.

Death Mountain Trail — Red Tektites jump at you if you get close: target one, wait for its leap, and press B without touching the stick for a vertical slash that hits it mid-air. Time it well and they're trivial; otherwise avoid them or use Deku Nuts.

keywords: Talk to the Goron by the sealed cavern
steps.detail: Up the trail, a rock-like creature stands by a large cracked boulder: a Goron, one of many on Death Mountain. The boulder behind him seals Dodongo's Cavern — the Gorons' main source of tasty rocks — courtesy of Ganondorf, the fiend!

Round the corner and continue upward. In the large area, keep to the right of the flag and stay left of the rolling Goron — if he hits you, he knocks you off the mountain. Enter the giant bannered entrance to Goron City.

keywords: Play Zelda's Lullaby on the carpet
steps.detail: Goron City is a stack of tiers full of doors and twisting corridors; the objective is the bottom level, so just jump all the way down. The big door there is closed — the nearby Goron gives the clue: stand on the carpet and play Zelda's Lullaby, and the Goron leader opens the door.

keywords: Open the Lost Woods shortcut (Bomb Flower corridor)
steps.detail: Light a Deku Stick on the torch in Darunia's chamber and run outside, lighting all the torches on the bottom floor to set the giant pot spinning. Follow the right wall, up the steps and around the corner into a corridor with boulders and Bomb Flowers — the not-so-obvious part: touch a Bomb Flower on either side with your lit stick, stand back, and enjoy the fireworks. The cleared corridor is Goron City's shortcut to the Lost Woods.

keywords: If you skipped Saria's Song: detour via the shortcut
steps.detail: Only if you don't have Saria's Song yet (chapter 3 covered it): take the shortcut into the Lost Woods and go left, left, forward, left, right to the Sacred Forest Meadow. Beat the Wolfos (shield up, stab after its attack or when its back turns), push past the Mad Scrubs (three seeds a shot — approach shielded, use Deku Nuts or sword), cross the maze to the ladder and stairs, beat the two Mad Scrubs at the archway, and Saria teaches you her song at the meadow's heart. Return along the wall-tops shortcut, then left, right, forward, right, right brings you back to Goron City.

Back at Darunia's chamber, play Saria's Song standing in front of him. After the boss man dances a jig, he turns serious but talkative: he'll give you the Spiritual Stone of Fire… if you kill the beasts in Dodongo's Cavern. As a free gift he hands over helpful jewelry — the Goron's Bracelet, needed to even enter the cavern.

keywords: Optional: Bomb Flower into the spinning urn
steps.detail: One more thing here: light the torches on the lowest tier (Deku Stick from Darunia's torch) to set the giant Goron urn spinning. Climb to the second-to-bottom tier, turn left, grab the Bomb Flower up the next steps, and run to toss it into the moving urn. The goal is for it to explode inside while the smiling face points at you — that makes a Piece of a Heart appear. Several tries likely; be patient.

keywords: Toss the Bomb Flower off the overhang
steps.detail: From Goron City's entrance head right rather than back down. On the little overhang wait a lone Goron and a Bomb Flower. Pick up the Bomb Flower and toss it straight ahead over the ledge — it lands beside the giant boulder below and blows open the entrance to Dodongo's Cavern. (Why couldn't the rock-people ten times stronger than you do that?) Side note: from up here you could backflip at an angle over the short Bomb Flower fence onto the cavern entrance's roof and grab a Piece of Heart (#26) early — not the intended way, so the walkthrough saves it for later, but the option is open.

keywords: Optional: bugs on the soft soil by the entrance
steps.detail: Just outside the cavern is a soft soil spot — drop bugs to bring out a Gold Skulltula. No bugs nearby: hop back to the Graveyard and grab some from under the rock near its entrance.

Straight ahead inside is an odd-looking wall: blow it up with one of the nearby Bomb Flowers to reveal the whole of Dodongo's Cavern. The place is full of lava and fire enemies, so equip the Hylian Shield so your little Deku edition doesn't burn — Navi hints the same as you head forward.

keywords: Left side of the main room
steps.detail: Main Room — ahead stands a Beamos, a statue enemy with a giant eyeball (killable with Bomb Flowers, best avoided for now). Reach the left side of the room by hopping via the Beamos platform, or by running the dry ground at the very edge of the left platform beside the lava, circling around back to a ladder. Toss the lonely Bomb Flower at the funny-looking wall to reveal a large chest: the Dungeon Map. The next funny-looking wall hides a Business Scrub selling Deku Shields, in case you burn yours.

keywords: Bomb the far wall, go through
steps.detail: Work to the opposite side of the room the same two ways. The first bombable wall there holds only a Gossip Stone (unusable yet). Pick up the Bomb Flower, kill the Beamos on this platform, then use the regrowing Bomb Flower to blow the farther funny-looking wall behind where the Beamos stood. Go through the revealed passage.

Baby Dodongos lurk in this small passage: watch them, slash when close, and stand clear when they explode. Halfway across, a bombable wall on the right leads to a Gold Skulltula — you can carefully lure a Baby Dodongo to blow it open early, but most will wait (we return with Bombs later). Up the ledge, a barred door sits over a switch that must stay pressed: push/pull one of the Armos statues onto it and go through.

Through the Keese hallway (kill the two if you like), the door shuts behind you: a face-off with two Lizalfos. They take turns attacking, so handle one at a time — Z-target the aggressor, defend with the shield, and smack it between attacks; shield up when it jumps over you. Avoid the lava. Both doors open when they fall; snag the jar goodies if needed and head through the next door.

keywords: Light the torches with a Deku Stick
steps.detail: In the next hallway the corner has a bombable wall — the nearby Bomb Flowers open it, but it's just a Business Scrub selling Deku Sticks. Ahead: Dodongos, large fire-breathing lizards vulnerable only in the tail. Bait the fire attack, circle behind, smack the tail and get clear — they spin fast. A Deku Stick Jump Attack on the tail one-shots them; the boring way is Slingshot shots between attacks. Three to beat. The next door is barred: three unlit torches plus one lit one — Deku Stick from the room's start, light them all along the way, and go through.

keywords: Upper ledge: step on the switch
steps.detail: Back in the center room on an upper ledge: run forward and step on the switch to open a barred door across the way, then hop down and cross to it.

keywords: Beat the Armos, take the Compass
steps.detail: The Big Steps — this giant room looks awfully suspicious; first, the Compass (say yes, say yes!): pick up a Bomb Flower and blow the odd wall on the left, revealing a door. Inside is your first living Armos: run up, touch it to wake it, then back off — a Deku Nut then a Jump Attack works well; dodge or Hylian-Shield its trampling explosion. Open the large chest for the Compass, then head back.

Bomb Flowers line both sides of the big pillar, with one suspicious gap in the middle. Place the lone Bomb Flower from near the Compass door into the gap so they all blow at once — the firepower sinks the pillar into the ground, revealing gigantic steps to the upper area. How… convenient…

keywords: Kill the Skullwalltulas + Gold Skulltula on the vines
steps.detail: Climb the big steps (takes forever, doesn't it?) and circle around. At the vines, stop to kill the Skullwalltulas — and a Gold Skulltula with them, oh happy day! Token in hand, whip around and go through the next door.

keywords: Fire Keese, then pull the dead Armos
steps.detail: Armos Room — the hexagonal room holds Fire Keese; keep the Hylian Shield equipped so they can't burn the other one, and pick them off with the Slingshot. Find the platform side with the ladder, pull out the one Armos statue that ISN'T alive from in front of it, climb up and hit the switch: both doors open and the next one is pointed out. Jump down and go through.

keywords: Blade Traps below: block, rupee chest, bomb the ledge wall
steps.detail: Back in the main room above the entrance, two more Fire Keese — don't let them knock you off; head straight across the bridge (with its deliberate missing chunk, JERKS!). In the Trap Room, look down from the edge first: Blade Traps. Jump straight ahead between the two pillars; Navi notes you can Z-target to peek around corners. Push on to the Stone Block — pull it back for a Recovery Heart in the hole, then pull it to the nearby platform and climb up: a little chest holds a Red Rupee (20). Grab the Bomb Flower and toss it at the funny wall above the ladder (it regrows if you miss; you can also hold it until it flashes fast and lob it to blow mid-air). Jump over and continue.

keywords: Shield through the Baby Dodongo hall
steps.detail: Flame Puzzle 1 — the platform to the next hallway burns with a raging flame, watched by a suspicious Eye Switch: shoot it with the Slingshot to douse the fire momentarily, jump across, and shield your way down the hallway infested with Baby Dodongos (they jump right over a raised Hylian Shield). Enter the door at the end.

keywords: Beat the two Lizalfos on the platforms
steps.detail: Lizalfos Room, upper — two Lizalfos on little platforms over lava. More confined, same fight; it just makes Jump Attacks harder. When both fall, the next door opens; Recovery Hearts wait on the side platform (look — it's the top of the room you fought them in earlier!). Head through.

keywords: Two Eye Switches douse two flames
steps.detail: Flame Puzzle 2 — shoot the Eye Switch like last time… and the next platform still burns! From the first platform look left: another Eye Switch hides in the cubby. Shoot it to douse the second flame. Onward, to victory!

Back above the Trap Room: jump to the next platform, climb the ledge on the right, and open the large chest for the dungeon item — Bombs! Now we can blow everything up. (The bombable wall a level down? DON'T — just Business Scrubs selling Deku Nuts and Seeds.) Continue through the next opening.

In the final stretch of the main room's second floor, step on the switch to extend one of the elevator lifts to the upper floor — a shortcut. Ahead, a stone block's poetry hints at what's next. Head right onto the bridge; the bombable wall there holds just a small chest with a Red Rupee (20) — and a replacement Kokiri Shield if you lost yours.

keywords: Drop Bombs through the bridge holes
steps.detail: As the poem said: make the giant Dodongo's eyes red. The bridge conveniently has holes right above the eyes — stand at each hole's edge and drop a Bomb straight down (press the C button twice), wait for the blast, then do the other eye. With both eyes lit, the mouth lowers, revealing a door. To get down, go the long way, jump to your doom, or the author's way: onto the skull, then the platform.

keywords: Optional: Gold Skulltula behind the corridor wall
steps.detail: Before entering the skull, collect the Skulltula skipped earlier: facing the Dodongo skull, take the farthest doorway on the right back into the Baby Dodongo corridor. Follow the right wall past the Baby Dodongos to the bombable wall, blow it open with your own Bombs, and in the small room deal with three Keese (try tossing a Bomb at them for fun) and take the Gold Skulltula token — a Jump Attack or backflip reaches it. Then return and enter the throat of the beast; so scary!

keywords: S-Shaped Room: bomb the ledge wall
steps.detail: Inside, the boss door can't be opened yet. Hop down into the square room (Fire Keese optional), climb up on the right and follow the long hallway to the "S"-shaped room. Kill its two obnoxious Fire Keese first, cross the room over the blocks, and bomb the wall on the ledge on the far side — toss a Bomb onto the ledge, or hold it until it's about to blow and lob it. Go through the door.

keywords: Optional: Armos guards a Gold Skulltula
steps.detail: A lone living Armos guards the next Gold Skulltula. Defeat it, snag the token, and return to the previous room.

keywords: Block onto the switch — boss door opens
steps.detail: The Fire Keese are back; re-clear if needed. Use the ledge you climbed earlier to get on top of the high wall, run along it and drop into the next area (a Fairy hides in one of the jars to the left — bottle it if you like). Down the hallway you're back in the square room, now with a stone block: push it off the ledge, hop down, kill the revived Fire Keese, then push/pull the block onto the switch in the center hole. The boss door stays open — go on in.

keywords: Bomb the floor patch, refill Bombs
steps.detail: In the boss antechamber, bomb the funny-looking patch on the floor and open the chest for Bombs (topping you up). Fall down the hole to begin the battle.

keywords: Bomb his mouth while he inhales
steps.detail: King Dodongo just looks big — his size makes for awkward, avoidable attacks: a fire breath he charges slowly, and a rolling pass over half the arena (usually right after the breath). Stand near the lava or tuck into a corner to dodge both; the roll can also simply be blocked with the shield. Per Navi, "give him a shock": toss a Bomb or Bomb Flower into his mouth while he inhales — it blows in his stomach and stuns him; slash away, then sidestep toward the lava to dodge the follow-up roll and reset. Four or five hits kill him; with planning you can throw the Bomb, draw the sword as he rears, and land a Jump Attack the instant he hits the ground for extra damage.

missable.deadline: During this fight — Bombalicious requires beating King Dodongo before he breathes fire without using Bombs from your bag; Dangerously Delicious adds no damage taken, with 3 hearts only

steps.detail: With the old king defeated, grab the Heart Container left behind, then enter the blue portal to leave the dungeon.

keywords: Receive the Goron's Ruby from Darunia
steps.detail: Outside the dungeon Darunia greets you, plants you on your face with a friendly pat, laments Ganondorf's boulder, and declares you two Sworn Brothers (whatever that means — ooo, Darunia knows how to glow). He gives you Goron's Ruby, the second Spiritual Stone, and urges you to visit the Great Fairy at the top of the mountain. Then two Gorons drop in seeking a huddle of love — run from the Goron hugs, Link!

keywords: Optional: bomb the wall — Gold Skulltula
steps.detail: Heading back toward Kakariko you'll pass a funny-looking chunk of wall with Gold Skulltula sounds behind it: bomb it, kill the spider, and climb the oddly climbable stone surface. On the way to Goron City there's a second bombable wall hiding a chest with a Purple Rupee.

keywords: Bomb-stop the Hot Rodder Goron in the north cave
steps.detail: A large rolling Goron now careens through the city: the "Hot Rodder Goron". Don't get too close — he'll run you over. Stop his crazy awesome rolling with a well-timed Bomb while he's under the closed-in, cave-like area on the city's north side, and (though annoyed) he hands over a nifty upgrade.

keywords: Optional: bomb through the rock maze
steps.detail: With plenty of Bombs, head to the top of the city: three rocks block the west path — bomb them (or tightrope the ropes to the pedestal; fun, not worth the time). The short path beyond leads to a rock maze. Ignore the silver boulders (nothing to do with them yet) and blast the regular ones — stay right for the first half, then middle — until you find all the treasures: small chests with a Purple Rupee (50) each at the end, and a crate to roll into for a Gold Skulltula. Then leave Goron City. Note: the Walking on a Tightrope achievement requires reaching this boulder room's chests via the tightrope ropes WITHOUT bombing the three-rock entrance blockade.

missable.deadline: Once you bomb the three-rock blockade it's gone — Walking on a Tightrope requires opening a chest in Goron City's boulder room without destroying that entrance blockade (take the ropes instead)

keywords: Bomb the boulders at the split
steps.detail: Time to ascend to the Great Fairy as Darunia said. Return to where the trail splits and take the highest point so far: of the three boulders, two block the way. Blow up the first, then hold a Bomb until it's about to explode and toss it at the second. Jump over and collect the Red Rupee (20) underneath. Climbing up, another boulder hides a hole with a cow in it, should you desire milk (Epona's Song).

keywords: Clear the Skullwalltulas, climb to the summit
steps.detail: Along the path the volcano abruptly erupts! Hylian Shield equipped, hold R to wait out each burst and dash between the gaps in flying magma and rocks — it stops once you near the other side. Kill the Skullwalltulas with the Slingshot before climbing, and it's smooth sailing to the top, where the Owl waits (ignore him for now) beside a hole in the mountain on the right.

keywords: Optional: crate Gold Skulltula at the entrance
steps.detail: Death Mountain Crater — whew, is it hot in here! We're not really supposed to be here yet, and the heat timer will kill you if it runs out, but two quick grabs: first, roll into the crate at the entrance for a Skulltula spider.

keywords: Optional: climb down the cliff cubby — Heart Piece
steps.detail: Re-enter to reset the heat timer, run to the ledge and line up facing the Triforce platform below, then run at the cliff — you'll flip around and grab the climbable wall. Climb down to the small cubby mid-cliff holding a Heart Piece. You can't get back in time, so jump into the lava (really — better than waiting out the timer): you reappear at the entrance minus one heart. Exit.

Atop the mountain, swerve around the bird and lay a Bomb against the funny-looking wall to reveal a hidden cave: the first Great Fairy Fountain. Stand on the Triforce symbol and play Zelda's Lullaby. A large, scandalous woman appears, giggles, makes idle chat, and grants your first magic ability: the Spin Attack. She also reminds you to visit her friend near Hyrule Castle for another power.

keywords: Owl ride down to Kakariko's roof
steps.detail: Back outside, speak with the owl (yawn) and stand underneath him when he's done for a ride down to Kakariko — this rooftop shortcut sets up a hidden prize.

keywords: Optional: roof → thatch overhang → cow house Heart Piece
steps.detail: Don't jump off the building yet — a Piece of a Heart is reachable from this roof (the only other way is the Hookshot, which we don't have). Sneak to the right side of the roof and peek over the edge above the Cucco Lady's pen: jump down to the thatch overhang and turn around to find a hole in the house wall. Inside, next to a cow behind bars (why is there a cow in a house…?), sits the Piece of a Heart. If you jumped off by mistake: until you leave the area, playing the Sun's Song restarts the area and puts you back where you entered — in this case, on the roof.

keywords: Bombchu Bowling: hit the wall targets
Prizes cycle: Bombchus, Heart Piece, Bombs, Bomb Bag, Purple Rupee
steps.detail: With Bombs in hand, the Bombchu Bowling Alley in the Market is now open (same side as the Shooting Gallery, between the exits to Hyrule Field and Hyrule Castle). No time limit — take your time dropping Bombchus at angles past the obstacles into the wall targets; they climb walls, so the sides are usable too. Area 1: aim dead straight, wait for a clear path. Area 2: angle Link about 45 degrees. Area 3 has a giant chicken parked in a ditch: drop a Bombchu when it's centered and the small one is elsewhere, or stand off to a side and wait for the big one to move opposite. The prizes cycle in order — Bombchus, Heart Piece, Bombs, Bomb Bag, Purple Rupee (50) — starting from a random one, so keep playing (Rupee refills at the guardhouse nearby) or come back later for the ones you miss.

In the Hyrule Castle area, climb the vines and go straight ahead, jump down and bomb the boulder behind the "Dead End" sign, then crawl through the hole. Inside, play Zelda's Lullaby to summon the Great Fairy of Magic, who grants a most useful spell. NOTE: to acquire Din's Fire you must already have visited the Great Fairy at the very top of Death Mountain.

keywords: Optional: bomb the hidden grotto by Lake Hylia's entrance
steps.detail: One more Heart Piece, back in Hyrule Field: in the southwest, right by the Lake Hylia entrance, in the middle of the four square fences, the Shard of Agony chimes (Stone of Agony rumbles) — a secret. Drop a bomb dead center to uncover a hidden grotto; the Business Scrub inside sells a Piece of Heart for 10 Rupees. Sweet deal!

keywords: Bomb the boulder near the Meadow entrance
Buy the Deku Nut upgrade (40 Rupees)
steps.detail: One last goody: cross the Lost Woods to the final area just before the Sacred Forest Meadow entrance, where a boulder can be blasted away with a bomb. Inside wait a pair of Business Scrubs — the first sells a Deku Nut Upgrade for 40 Rupees.

missable.deadline: Child era, Lost Woods — Stocking Nuts requires purchasing a Deku Nuts upgrade here

keywords: Follow the river to Zora's River
steps.detail: Next destination: Zora's Domain, on the east side of Hyrule Field between Kokiri Forest and Kakariko Village. From the Hyrule Town drawbridge, follow the river upstream into Zora's River. Run forward for another owl encounter — and this one matters: at the end of this area you must play Zelda's Lullaby to enter Zora's Domain. After he flies off, roll into the tree on your left for a Gold Skulltula token.
Boulders block the way onward — blast them, I say! After the rubble clears you'll see a Cucco straight ahead; make note of it and round the corner.

keywords: Plant one in the soil here
steps.detail: A chubby fellow eating from a bag sells you some of his "Magic Beans". The price rises with every purchase and this is the ONLY place to get them, so fork over all the Rupees you have — you can probably afford at least three. Test one on the soft soil next to him; it does nothing for a while yet, but it'll more than pay for itself in time. Oddly, this is the only soft soil spot in the whole game with no Gold Skulltula, so don't waste bugs.

Want more Magic Bean money right now? Run back to Kakariko Village: just beyond the first tree, the Shard of Agony sounds a secret. Drop a bomb to open a hidden grotto, defeat the two ReDeads inside, and the chest holds a Gold Rupee — 200 Rupees, plenty of Magic Beans for now.

Time to head up the river — and for the secrets on the way, go back and pick up that suspicious Cucco (if it ever touches the water, it reappears back here). Jump to the opposite side of the river and follow it to the corner, where an Octorok lurks in the water: keep running to dodge its rocks and jump the river again at its shortest point.

keywords: Optional: Cucco-jump to the platform Heart Piece
steps.detail: Follow the path left, hop the little jump and work around — a water-dwelling Tektite variant swims here. With the chicken in hand, jump at the platform ahead, drop the chicken mid-air as you touch the platform, and you'll grab hold: pull up and jump for the prize, a Heart Piece!

keywords: Ferry the Cucco up the platforms
steps.detail: Hop down, avoiding the Blue Tektites. For yet another Heart Piece, carry the chicken along to your right: at each platform, toss the chicken up, climb yourself, pick it up again — turn right through the water and repeat. Past the two platform sets, go left across the water and over the bridge into the open area of twisting platforms; hop to the next platform on the right when you get the chance and follow it to the top, closest to the waterfall.

keywords: Optional: Cucco-glide to the lonely Heart Piece
steps.detail: From the highest point, turn around: a Heart Piece sits alone on a corner platform. With the chicken you can jump all the way to it and claim it early. Mwahaha!

keywords: Optional: night — Gold Skulltula #29 by the ladder
steps.detail: Play the Sun's Song for night: near the waterfall at the river's end, a Gold Skulltula appears by the ladder leading out of the water. Hop into the shallow water, shoot it down with the Slingshot and collect the token.

keywords: Play Zelda's Lullaby on the grate
steps.detail: At long last, stand on the square grate-looking thing on the top platform in front of the waterfall and play Zelda's Lullaby, as the Owl said. The waterfall parts, revealing the entrance to Zora's Domain. Nice work, Moses!
Familiarize yourself quickly: the spiral ramp in the middle goes up and down (or just jump in the water below to descend fast), and there's a shop nearby — nothing too exciting in stock.

keywords: Chest behind the waterfall: Heart Piece
steps.detail: Head upstairs to where fat King Zora sits (rock on, dude). A Piece of a Heart is easily earned with the torches nearby — there's a Deku Stick in the jars along the ramp if you're out. The order: run down the stairs and light the first torch; continue downstairs and light the torch in front of the shop; light the torch in the water near the circle of rocks; then the two torches behind the waterfall. With all five lit at once, a large chest appears behind the waterfall containing the Heart Piece.

keywords: Diving Game: grab every Rupee in 50s
steps.detail: From the top, take a left at King Zora — the water leads to the top of the waterfall, where a Zora runs a mini-game. Pay him and he tosses Rupees into the water below with a 50-second timer: jump off the waterfall and hold A to dive repeatedly, swimming underwater to grab two or more at once (Z redirects you if disoriented). Miss the timer and they vanish. The prize for getting them all is the Silver Scale — plus the Rupees themselves; occasionally he even throws pink 50s or a very rare gold one. Keep playing until you clear it, then collect your prize up top.
Silver Scale in hand, jump back into the water and dive to the deepest part, where a hole waits — one of several shortcuts between places in Hyrule. Swim through it.

keywords: Dive for the bottle: Ruto's letter
steps.detail: You surface in Lake Hylia. Near the water's edge by the stone pillar ruins, Navi flies to something in the water and glows green: swim over and dive for it — a bottle with a letter inside, from the missing Zora princess! The bottle itself stays unusable until the letter reaches King Zora. (If you skipped Lake Hylia's goodies, chapter 3 covers them.) Swim back through the hole to Zora's Domain.

keywords: Show the letter to King Zora
steps.detail: Upstairs, stand on the platform and pull out the bottled letter to show the king. After a short conversation and one of the lengthiest butt-hops ever witnessed, you're allowed through to Jabu-Jabu's fountain. One more thing first, though.

keywords: Bottle a fish from the shallows
steps.detail: Downstairs (or dive off the waterfall), scoop a fish from the shallow water with your newly emptied bottle — they hang around a circle of stones and are easy to spot. (The Shop sells one for 200 Rupees… or not.) Fish in hand, head back up and through the passage King Zora was hiding.

keywords: Optional: Gold Skulltula #30 in the lone tree
steps.detail: At Zora's Fountain, swim to the small piece of land in the southeast corner (bottom right of the map) and roll into the lone tree to pop out a Gold Skulltula.

Against the wall stand two boulders. The silver one can't be moved yet, but a crack in the wall hints it's bombable: lay a Bomb next to the silver boulder and enter the revealed cave. Play Zelda's Lullaby on the Triforce symbol for the Great Fairy of Magic, who gifts Farore's Wind — mark a spot, teleport back to it whenever you want. Dungeons only, but still cool. (Note: you should now have 30 Gold Skulltula tokens; the Giant's Wallet reward waits in Kakariko, but it's totally out of the way right now.)

Time for the dungeon. Walk to Jabu-Jabu, the great fish lord the Zora worship — one of the Zora below mentioned bringing a fish offering. Pull out the bottled fish and lay it on the ground: you're inhaled. Something to write home about, no?

Mouth (Entrance) — despite the dramatic clip, you can leave anytime by… walking through his teeth. Why of course. A step forward brings two Octoroks out of the water: reflect their shots with the Deku Shield, or dodge and use the Slingshot.

keywords: Shoot the ceiling nob to open the veiny door
steps.detail: The veiny door ahead won't open on its own. The Shaboms (bubbles) are pretty harmless — a Deku Nut clears bunches of them best. Where the corridor narrows, Navi flies to a small nob on the ceiling: shoot it with the Slingshot to open the door.

keywords: Skirt the Biri, take the far door
steps.detail: Throat (Elevator Room) — your first Biri, a floating electric jellyfish. Best avoided for now (Deku Nuts kill them if you insist). Work around and enter the opposite door.

Upper Stomach — triumphant music! The Zora Princess… who claims to know nothing of any letter and certainly doesn't want your help. After a quick smack-talk she falls down a hole. Sigh… go on after her.

Down below, talk with her repeatedly until she stops complaining. She mentions her "precious stone" — surely the Spiritual Stone of Water we're after — then crouches into a ball so you can carry her around. Lazy fishface… If you drown her or leave a room without her, she reappears here. Pick her up before too many Biri close in and take the nearby door.

Switch Puzzle — the hallway holds Shaboms (wait them out, sword them, toss the princess at them for great fun, or one Deku Nut for all). Around the corner, jump into the lower water, wary of the Stingers: kill them now while the water is low — they're maddening to dodge while swimming, and tossing the princess at them while burrowed one-shots them.

keywords: Toss Ruto up first, then the water switch
steps.detail: You can't climb the far side, so the pit's middle switch must raise the water — but first toss Ruto up to the other side where she's safe (sink her and she reappears in the previous room, angry). After stepping on the switch, grab the Gold Skulltula token on the side wall (shoot it, climb up for it) and the fairy in the jars above for a bottle.

keywords: Climb up, shoot the nob door
steps.detail: Use the raised water to climb the other side, pick up the princess and continue down the Shabom-infested hallway to another "closed" door — again, shoot the dangly ceiling nob and head through.

Back in the Throat, set the princess down fast and shield-reflect the Octorok's attack. Need Deku Nuts? A Business Scrub below the hole straight ahead sells them for 20 Rupees (reflect his nuts first). Then pick Ruto up, wait for the spiked platform, ride it back to the earlier small hallway, hop off and enter the door to the room where you first met her.

From now on, a lost Ruto reappears in this room. Cross to the door ignored until now, careful of the Biri and the new ceiling-dropping Bari — the latter splits into three Biri when killed. Enter the door.

keywords: Right-hand switch: weigh it with Ruto
steps.detail: Intestines — a branching hallway of doors you can't open yet, patrolled by Tailpasarans, electric floaters you can't even hurt yet. Head right and jump on the switch: Ruto's weight is needed to press it fully. Go through the door it opens.
Set Ruto down near the entrance and slaughter the four Stingers hiding in the floor. A large chest appears with the Boomerang! Equip it, pick Ruto back up and exit.

keywords: Park Ruto on the far switch
steps.detail: Now everything here can be hurt — set Ruto down and clear the Tailpasarans if you like. Cross to the other switch-door on the opposite side. This switch pops back up when stepped off, so set the princess down ON it and go through without her. What a shame. =)
Your first Parasitic Tentacle: Z-target, get close, and circle while throwing the Boomerang. A few good hits kill it, leaving a large chest with the Dungeon Map. Exit the room.

Ruto is annoyed you left her — and from here on, most rooms make her vanish if left behind, which is fine; bring her or don't. For the Compass, round the corner into the nearby door (Navi notes this half's Tentacle is gone): the small room floods with Shaboms on a 40-second timer — fail and the room resets with some health lost. Z-target with the Boomerang, or a daring spin attack for bunches. Pop them all and the Compass chest appears. Woot.

Back across the massive hallway room to where the Boomerang was found — the room right next to it is now open. Inside, another lone Parasitic Tentacle; simple at this point. Kill it and head for the middle (final) hallway.

keywords: Kill the Biri first, then the Green Tail
steps.detail: The last Tentacle is ringed by Biri — clear them first or it slaps you into them. With all of them down, the door opens; head back to the Ruto Room.

keywords: Drop down the freed top-left hole
steps.detail: The hole nearest the top-left (opposite where you first saw Ruto) used to have a large tentacle through it — now gone. Fall down it to land on a platform you couldn't reach before.

Two Gold Skulltulas cling to the wall here — exactly what the Boomerang is for: defeat them and snag both tokens (the closer, then the further one). Then take the only hallway on this platform and enter the door.

keywords: Throw Ruto to the center platform
steps.detail: Big Octo's Lair — Ruto squirms and demands to be thrown onto the platform in the center. Do so, and a short clip plays: she gets the Spiritual Stone of Water! Lame… take a step forward and… oh no… that's really… too bad… =)

keywords: Stun with Boomerang, slash his back
steps.detail: This giant Octorok circles a spinning spiked platform of doom, running about as fast as you. He turns around when he rams you, and sometimes burrows to reappear on the opposite side (watch the spikes to read his direction). Stun him anywhere with the Boomerang; he's only sword-vulnerable from behind. Hard way: chase him along the spikes until close enough, Z-target, Boomerang, slash. Easy way: he turns when he hits you or (sometimes) when stunned — so stand still, get mowed over on purpose, stun, slash, repeat; or stun him, let the stun wear off, re-stun while he faces away, smack. Four slashes (or two Jump Attacks) to his weak spot and Big Octo is a Big Loser. He usually drops three hearts; grab them, ride the center platform up, bottle the fairy in the jar if needed, and take the only exit, killing Biri on the way.

keywords: Freeze the red jellies, hop across
steps.detail: Red Jelly Puzzle — wiggly electric jelly blobs that hate you. Kill the Octorok below first if you like, then from the platform by the entrance, Boomerang the first angry tofu to freeze it into a usable platform. Jump on, freeze the second, and make the next door.

Throat again — jump onto the lone platform across the way; it lowers to connect an old platform to an unexplored door. The door's switch pops back up when released, and Ruto's gone — so grab one of the nearby boxes and weigh it down with that.

keywords: Optional: Gold Skulltula #34 on the vines
steps.detail: In this last room before the boss, kill the Biri quickly, then find the Gold Skulltula on the vines at the far side. Kill it, climb the vines (snagging the token on the way) and get on top.

keywords: Boomerang the switch through the web
steps.detail: Up top hangs another ceiling switch behind a spider-web-like barrier: Z-target and throw the Boomerang — it may take a few tries to get the distance right. The boss door opens; head in when ready.

Phase 1: Boomerang the three ceiling appendages sucking energy from Lord Jabu-Jabu, one at a time, circling to dodge the electric beams. Phase 2: he orbits jellyfish around his body with a dangerous electric connection — weave close and Boomerang the main body to break the link, making the jellies killable (sword and Din's Fire work; Deku Nuts and Boomerang are best); re-stun the body before it revives, repeat. Phase 3: his last line spins fast around the body as he zips about — keep distance, keep throwing the Boomerang; once through, he can now shoot while stunned, so rush in and toss a Deku Nut at the body to clear the ring at once. Phase 4: with all Biri gone he zips and shoots faster — keep moving, stun with the Boomerang, and slash the main body; he periodically sinks to protect himself, so back off until he rises. Ten sword slashes or so and Barinade explodes.

missable.deadline: During this fight — Rolling Thunder requires beating Barinade without getting hit; Race Against the Clock requires under 1:51 with 3 hearts only

keywords: Join Ruto in the blue light
steps.detail: Who knew electric fish thingies grew tons of zits when they died… Grab the Heart Container and meet Ruto in the blue light for a quick scolding about how long you took. Sure am glad I saved you…

Outside, Ruto changes her mind about you and muses about making Link her personal… whatever you answer, she hints it's an engagement gift between you two. Whoa nelly! Either way: the Spiritual Stone of Water is yours! (The water does look cool right here.) You end up floating in Zora's Fountain.

keywords: Optional: night — Gold Skulltula #35 by the log
steps.detail: Play the Sun's Song for night and swim toward the exit: right before the passage back to Zora's Domain, a log arches out of the water on Jabu-Jabu's left. Stand on it, kill the Gold Skulltula crawling on the wall, and collect the token with the Boomerang.

The Boomerang opens up goodies all over the world — first, way back inside the Great Deku Tree: the never-entered room at the northwest corner of the first basement. Burn the spider-web, blast the boulder with a bomb, and use the Boomerang to snag the Gold Skulltula token on the wall inside.

At Lon Lon Ranch at night: one Gold Skulltula on the window of the building on the left side as you enter — defeat it and collect with the Boomerang; another at the far southwest of the map, higher up on the edge — same drill.

keywords: Optional: Gold Skulltula #39 (cow grotto, Din's Fire)
steps.detail: At the west end of Hyrule Field near the Gerudo Valley entrance is a circle of rocks; the Shard of Agony chimes in the middle. Bomb open the underground grotto, burn all the spider webs with Din's Fire, and behind the cow near the top waits a Gold Skulltula — Boomerang the token.

keywords: Optional: night — Gold Skulltula #40 by the water hole
steps.detail: Entering Gerudo Valley, walk forward to the wooden ramp and turn right: water pours from a hole in the wall, and at night a Gold Skulltula sits there.

keywords: Optional: night — Gold Skulltula #41 on the Lab wall
steps.detail: At the northwest side of Lake Hylia, walk onto the bridge near the Lakeside Laboratory and look at the building at night: a Skulltula. Defeat it and Boomerang the token.

keywords: Optional: Gold Skulltula #42 (tree grotto near Kakariko)
steps.detail: In Hyrule Field near the Kakariko entrance, just north of the small bridge between Castle Town and Kakariko, stands a tree where the Shard of Agony chimes. In the grotto below wait a Big Skulltula and, higher on the wall, a Gold Skulltula.
On the wall at the southeast corner of the Graveyard, at night — you'll need the Boomerang to retrieve this token.

Back in Dodongo's Cavern, in the room with the massive explodable staircase — the long route: ride the tall pillar up to the second floor, head west a room, drop down and climb the ladder at the southwest before returning to the main room; cross the large bridge to the Armos statue room, push the statue, climb the ladder, hit the switch, and you're back at the giant staircase. The Skulltula hides in a cubby at the TOP of the staircase: go around the left side and climb the vines, or jump down onto the steps themselves. Boomerang required for the token.

At the House of Skulltula: the 40-token reward is kind of lame — Bombchus (you may already have some from Bombchu Bowling). If you never collected the 30-token reward, do it now: the Giant's Wallet, holding up to 500 Rupees.

keywords: Catch-up: Boomerang the 2 river Heart Pieces
steps.detail: Rush back toward Castle Town. If you've followed the walkthrough, the two Zora's River Heart Pieces are already yours; if not, the Boomerang trivializes them now: one sits on the little ledge near the waterfall to Zora's Domain (throw from a nearby platform), the other about halfway through the area atop a skinny mid-water platform (use the ramping platform beside it). Then swim along the river — mash B to speed up — for free Rupees and record time back to Hyrule Field.

Three Spiritual Stones in hand, head back toward Hyrule Castle to see Princess Zelda — but the drawbridge is up in broad daylight and dark clouds loom. The movie begins: it's the dream from the game's start. The drawbridge lowers, Impa rides out on a white horse with Zelda at her side — and the girl tosses the Ocarina of Time toward you, landing it in the moat. Then Ganondorf, King of the Gerudo Thieves, plays twenty questions and zaps Link with glowy purple-and-gold stuff before riding off laughing: he attacked the castle, killed the king, and hunts Zelda to enter the Sacred Realm and claim the Triforce.

keywords: Dive the moat for the Ocarina of Time
steps.detail: Jump into the moat and dive for the Ocarina of Time — the moving water may take a couple of tries. On surfacing with it (TA-DA!), a vision of Princess Zelda teaches you the Song of Time. You reappear beside the drawbridge (magic!) with Navi scolding you toward the Temple of Time.

keywords: Hear the dying guard's last words in the back alley
steps.detail: For Last Breath: now that you have the Ocarina of Time from Zelda, go to the Hyrule Castle Town back alley, where a mortally wounded guard can be talked to — hear his dying words for the hidden dialogue. This is a one-time window: once the Temple of Time cutscenes trigger (pulling the Master Sword), the guard never appears again.

missable.deadline: After getting the Ocarina of Time, before the Temple of Time cutscenes — Last Breath requires hearing the dying guard in the Castle Town back alley; he never appears again once you pull the Master Sword

keywords: Optional side quest: borrow the Keaton Mask (10R)
steps.detail: The Happy Mask side quest is entirely optional; it becomes available once Zelda's letter reaches the guard at the base of Death Mountain, and it leads to the Mask of Truth, which lets you talk to the Gossip Stones all over Hyrule. The loop: borrow a mask at the Happy Mask Shop in the Market, find it a suitable owner, and pay back the price. First up is the Keaton Mask, 10 Rupees — an easy one.

keywords: Sell the Keaton Mask to the gate guard (15R)
steps.detail: The guard at the base of Death Mountain spoke at length about wanting this mask for his kid: slap it on, talk to him, and he pays 15 Rupees for it — a 5 Rupee profit.
Back at the shop, the second mask you're eligible to borrow is the Skull Mask, 20 Rupees — one whose owner would be impossible to find from what people say alone…

keywords: Forest Stage: wear the Skull Mask
steps.detail: In the Lost Woods, go right, left, right, left, left to the head meadow; at the back, walk around where the butterflies are to find a secret hole — the Forest Stage. Inside, put on the Skull Mask and step forward: a bunch of Deku appear, appraise you, and follow you around excitedly until they fetch their leader, who gifts you the second Deku Stick upgrade. Nice!

keywords: Sell the Skull Mask to the Skull Kid (10R)
