steps.detail: The mask's actual buyer is one of the Skull Kids near the Lost Woods entrance: turn left as you enter, step on the tree stump, put the Skull Mask on and speak with him. He pays 10 Rupees — a 10 Rupee loss on the deal. Oh well.
The third mask looks just like a ReDead (you'll see soon enough) — 30 Rupees, and a fairly easy sale.

keywords: Sell the Spooky Mask to Dampé's fan (30R)
steps.detail: In the Kakariko Graveyard by day, a small kid stomps around with a stick — his life dream is to be scary like Dampé the gravekeeper. Flash him the Spooky Mask and he coughs up all 30 Rupees without blinking. Sheesh!

keywords: Optional: plant a Magic Bean in the soft soil
steps.detail: While you're here, walk up the left side of the Graveyard to a soft soil spot: planting a Magic Bean here pays off when we return later in the game.
The last mask is less obvious: the Bunny Hood, 50 Rupees — simple, but it requires running around.

keywords: Sell the Bunny Hood to the Running Man (500R+)
steps.detail: The Running Man jogs Hyrule Field constantly: he starts in the northeast corner and circles counter-clockwise, hugging the road around Lon Lon Ranch. Easiest intercept: enter the Field from the Market and follow the road right — you'll spot him by the time you pass the Ranch. Wait for his breather (play the Sun's Song) and talk to him wearing the Bunny Hood: he's heard it grants mythical speed and agility, and he pays over 500 Rupees — a shame, since that's more than your wallet holds. Watch him zip away crazy fast afterwards. Why couldn't it do that for us? (Play the sequel for that. =)

Return to the Happy Mask Shop and pay the 50 Rupees the Bunny Hood was worth. The Happy Mask Salesman now lets you borrow four new masks — three are just for looks, but the Mask of Truth lets you hear the thoughts of animals, people, and above all the Gossip Stones (the giggling, time-telling stones scattered across Hyrule; ZD's Gossip Stone section lists all their "wisdom"). Borrow the Mask of Truth.

keywords: Forest Stage: wear the Mask of Truth
steps.detail: One last snag before the journey continues: back to the Lost Woods' secret meadow and the Forest Stage (right, left, right, left, left, then the butterfly-hidden hole at the back). Wear the Mask of Truth on the stage: the Deku turn out to be terrified of it, but reward you anyway — out of fear? — by enhancing your Deku Nuts. Kinda depressing, but cool! =)

missable.deadline: Child era, Lost Woods Forest Stage — A Horrible Face requires obtaining the Deku Nuts upgrade from the Deku Scrub Brothers

steps.detail: With the three Spiritual Stones and the Ocarina of Time, head through the stairs between the Happy Mask Shop and the Potion Shop in the Market. In the gardens you can chat with the Gossip Stones through your new Mask of Truth for some useless information… moving right along — round the corner and enter the giant temple.

keywords: Place the 3 stones, play the Song of Time
steps.detail: Place the Spiritual Stones on the pillar before the Door of Time. When it opens, enter the inner chamber — and there it is: the Master Sword. Climb onto the pedestal and claim the evil-destroying blade as the pedestal glows and shimmers with blue light. But it turns out opening the Door of Time was Ganondorf's plan all along: he used you to reach the Sacred Realm. Link's spirit is sealed away… for seven long years.

Link wakes in a flowing blue temple where an old man introduces himself as Rauru, Sage of Light: you were destined to take up the Master Sword, but too young for the journey, so your spirit slept seven years while Ganondorf claimed the Triforce and turned Hyrule into a land of monsters. The way to overthrow him is to gather the powers of the six sages — Rauru being the first; he gives you the Light Medallion and returns you to the quiet, dark Temple of Time. There, Sheik introduces herself — a guide of sorts, fond of poetic legends — and tells you to visit Kakariko Village before tackling the Forest Temple.

Exiting the Temple of Time: Death Mountain looks even worse, the trees around the Temple are dead, and the Market is run down and crawling with ReDeads — you saw a few as a child; you'll see many more now. Nothing to do here yet, so head for the drawbridge. Right before Hyrule Field, the guardhouse on the left now houses the Ghost Shop with a sadistic owner: chat with him and store him away in memory for a later quest.

Hyrule Field is calmer now — no more Stalchildren or Peahats. Head for Lon Lon Ranch in the center. (This whole section is optional; Epona isn't required to finish the quest.) Talon got kicked out and Ingo, the dirty stable worker, now owns the ranch; Malon hides in the stables clutching a broom, afraid Ingo will mistreat the horses if she leaves.

Speak with Ingo at the corral and pay 10 Rupees for his pony ride. Don't take the horse he offers — play Epona's Song and the best horse comes to you. (No Epona's Song? Malon teaches it in the child era; you can return to childhood after the Forest Temple and pick up anything missed.) Jump both fences with A pressed shortly before each — 20 Rupees total, earning back your fee.

Target Ingo while riding Epona and accept his race. Race 1: he cheats with a second's head start but takes corners wide — use A for carrot bursts, don't burn them all early; get ahead and block him, or save carrots for a final surge. Race 2 (he wagers the horse): more vicious, he cuts you off when close — zoom ahead right at the start and stay there.
Beaten twice, Ingo locks you in the ranch. Get a running start and jump the fence or any of the shorter walls on any side — cue the triumphant clip of Epona leaping out of Lon Lon Ranch. Da-da!

Re-enter the ranch afterwards: Ingo is a timid worker again and Malon recognizes you. She runs a mini-game — two laps around the track on Epona, hopping fences, in under 50 seconds. Genuinely a little challenging; a few tries likely. Win, and you get a Cow. Yes, an actual Cow — it appears at your old house in Kokiri Forest. How it got up there is another question…

missable.deadline: Adult era, Lon Lon Ranch — Jump Over requires completing the obstacle course and obtaining the reward

steps.detail: Epona now crosses Hyrule Field fast (call her with her song; she's mostly limited to the Field). Ride to Kakariko and pass through to the Graveyard, as Sheik said. On the left side, the soft soil holds a plant — if you planted a Magic Bean there as a child (chapter 6 did). Ride it up to the ledge above and smash the wooden crate for a Piece of a Heart. Didn't plant it? You can return later.
Right next to the bean plant is a grave with flowers in front of it. Pull it back to reveal a hole and fall in — inside waits a familiar character…

Dampé's Amazing Cave Race — follow the dead gravekeeper's ghost and don't get left behind! First chunk: hug the left wall. After the first door, hug the right wall to the big room, take the right exit, then a left and a right (purple flames). After that door, keep along the right side; at the ledges, climb the left one or straight ahead, then follow the path to the end.

After, you know… not losing… Dampé congratulates you and invites you back sometime. Open the chest he leaves: the Hookshot! With this we can finally tackle the first temple. Head for the open doorway.

keywords: Play the Song of Time at the blue block
steps.detail: The door closes behind you, leaving a giant blue block. The game isn't clear here, but target it and Navi notes it resembles the symbols in the Temple of Time: play the Song of Time to clear it (technically it just "moves" elsewhere — only relevant to Master Quest puzzles).

keywords: Windmill: ride the gear to Heart Piece #19
steps.detail: Climb the ledge and run up the stairs into the top of the Windmill. Before hopping down, a Piece of a Heart sits on a nearby ledge: wait for the gears to turn your way, jump onto the spinning platform and ride it across. (Technically grabbable as a child with the Boomerang, but awkward — and we were coming here anyway.) Down below, Guru Guru can teach the Song of Storms right now, but it isn't needed until much later — the guide returns to it when the time comes. On to the Forest Temple!

The Hookshot opens up goodies all over the Overworld (Lake Hylia, Zora's Domain, Gerudo Valley, Goron City — covered in later chapters; even the Biggoron Sword, cheaper later). Right now: return to Dampé's grave and race him again. Finish in one minute or less and he rewards you with a piece of heart.

keywords: Optional: Hookshot to the rooftop man
steps.detail: Coming out of the Windmill, jump onto the fence straight ahead, then Hookshot onto the building just to the right. A man sits on this roof — speak with him for a Heart Piece. (Doable as a child with a strategic jump, but now is the appropriate time.)

keywords: Optional: night — Gold Skulltula #45 on Impa's roof
steps.detail: At the south end of the village next to Impa's House: Hookshot onto the Skulltula House roof, then onto Impa's roof. The Gold Skulltula appears here at night.

keywords: Optional: Gold Skulltula #46 behind the archway
steps.detail: Return to the Market and head to where Hyrule Castle used to be — it's Ganon's Castle now. Follow the path right past the archway, then turn around: the Skulltula clings to the backside. Hookshot it and grab the token.

(Routing note: the Fire Temple can actually be done before the Forest Temple — it's near Kakariko where the Hookshot was found; skip to the next chapter if you prefer that order.) Cross Hyrule Field to Kokiri Forest. Things have changed — monsters everywhere, everyone hiding in their houses with pointless things to say, and nobody recognizes you because you grew up (unlike them). The Big Deku Babas here fight exactly like their smaller kin, just tall and awkward.

keywords: Optional: night — Gold Skulltula #47 above the Twins' House
steps.detail: Play the Sun's Song for night: a Skulltula is audible near the house closest to the Deku Tree (other than the shop) — the Twins' House at the southeast of the forest. Hookshot the Gold Skulltula above the building.

keywords: Climb the vines to the Lost Woods
steps.detail: Nothing else to do in the plant-infested village — climb the vines up to the Lost Woods.
You remember the route: right, left, right… hey, it's our old friend "The Path Blocker!" Mido demands proof you know Saria — play Saria's Song and he steps aside.

keywords: Optional: night — Gold Skulltula #48 (bean plant or Bombchu)
steps.detail: In the very next area, take a left: a Skulltula waits at night, high up — ride the Magic Bean plant you planted there in the past up to the top and slash it. No plant? Alternatively a Bombchu will climb the wall and blow up the Skulltula sitting on top — Hookshot the token. (Or plant the bean later and return.)

keywords: Cross the woods: right, left, right, left, straight, left, right
steps.detail: The whole path through the Lost Woods: right, left, right, left, straight, left, right.

keywords: Sneak past and kill the Moblins
steps.detail: The maze is now patrolled by Moblins. They never leave their set paths — stay out of their line of sight, sneak around corners and dash to the next alcove until close enough to kill each one. The Hookshot is the best tool (the sword is vindicating, and bouncing Bombs off walls feels tricky =D). They're only really trouble from the pools at the end — the last Moblin walks an L-path, which can mean a long wait before you can leave the water and slip past.

keywords: Zig-zag in, slash the Club Moblin's feet
steps.detail: At the steps stands a giant one — a Club Moblin, for obvious reasons. Intimidating, but easy: zig-zag close, then slash repeatedly at his feet until he falls over and drops tons of Rupees. Yippee!

keywords: Learn the Minuet of Forest from Sheik
steps.detail: Up the steps, Link gazes at the stump where Saria used to sit — then Sheik appears out of nowhere (as usual), quotes more poetry, and teaches you a snazzy little song that teleports you here anytime you want. Awesome!

keywords: Optional: night — Gold Skulltula #49 on the east wall
steps.detail: One more Skulltula before the temple, high on a wall, night only: play the Sun's Song, then the Minuet of Forest to warp to the far end of the Meadow. Re-kill the Club Moblin, climb the ladder at the end to the higher ledge, work left and look up at the east wall — Hookshot the token.

keywords: Save, then Hookshot the branch into the temple
steps.detail: When ready, save and head to the end of the Meadow: Hookshot to the branch above the steps leading into the Forest Temple.

This temple can get confusing — doors with the forest symbol above them lead "forward", ones without lead "backwards". The entrance room throws two Wolfos at you at once: Z-target one so the other leaves you alone, strike when their backs turn (double damage — a Jump Attack to the tail one-shots them).

keywords: Optional: Gold Skulltula #50 on the vines
steps.detail: Before the door, climb the vine on the right and drop onto the tree on your left: kill the Skulltula on the vines (Hookshot) and claim the token, then walk the branches and jump to the other tree for a chest with a Small Key.

keywords: Hookshot the Big Skulltula, enter the Grand Hall
steps.detail: In the entry hallway, kill the Big Skulltula blocking the path — one Hookshot hit from either side kills these from now on. Enter the next door.

keywords: Gold Skulltula #51 by the north door
steps.detail: The Grand Hall: the four colorful Poe Sisters remove their flames and vanish into the temple's four corners — we'll get to them. Head straight across, climb the ledge and stairs, and right before the north door, snag the Skulltula on the wall to your right.
The small hallway holds a Blue Bubble — a flaming skull invulnerable to your sword until stunned or knocked with the shield. Dispose of it or avoid it, then take the forest-symbol door.

Locked in with two Stalfos — dangerous this early; they hit hard. Z-target one at a time, keep your guard up, jump clear when they leap, and stab at every opening. When both fall, the chest holds the second Small Key. Bottle the Fairy in the nearby jars for later if you don't need it now. Return to the main room.

keywords: Song of Time at the blue block (NW door)
steps.detail: Head for the northwest door, blocked by a Time Block (big blue cube) like the one in Kakariko: play the Song of Time beside it to clear it and enter.

A Big Deku Baba greets you face-first — kill it or sneak by, then go right. On the vines, Hookshot the two lower Skullwalltulas, then climb past the third — a few tries maybe; staying as far right as possible helps. Enter the door at the top.
Kill the lone Blue Bubble to make the big chest appear with the Dungeon Map, then take the symbol door onward.

keywords: Hookshot to the platform, hit the switch
steps.detail: Northeast ruins: kill the regular-sized Deku Baba (aww, so small and cute now!), Hookshot the symbol above the nearby platform to land on it, and step on the switch — the well water drains here and in the earlier open-air room (it's one and the same). Jump into the drained water and climb onto land. A Skulltula is grabbable now: Hookshot the long side of the small chest on the ledge to pull yourself up, and find it right around the corner. Then climb down the well.
Within the well, run to the other end and open the chest for a Small Key, then climb out on either side and head back to the main room.
With keys to spare, take the locked door on the Grand Hall's west side, kill the Skulltula in the hallway with the Hookshot, and go through the candle door.

Block Room — kill the Blue Bubble, climb the first two ladders and round the corner. The puzzle looks complex but has arrows on the floor: (1) pull the BLUE block out until it lines up with the wall corner, push it all the way back from the left side, then circle through the uncovered doorway and push it again until it clicks into a hole. (2) Climb the now-uncovered ladder and round the corner to the RED block: push it to the wall. (3) Back down, climb atop the BLUE block and jump to the hiding ledge on the right. (4) Round the corner, reunite with the RED block, and push it until it clicks into place too.

keywords: Two Blue Bubbles, ignore the eye switch
steps.detail: Up here: two Blue Bubbles to kill, a silver eye switch to ignore for now, and a locked door to open. Go on, do it! Beyond lies the twisting west corridor — whoa. Just… whoa… Go through.

The strange little room beyond sits at a funky angle (we'll return at another angle later). Navi warns of a Wallmaster — don't let it grab you or it deals a whole heart and drags you back to the temple entrance! Keep moving: jump to the next platform, turn right, jump again. In Joelle's room beyond, nothing can be done yet — ignore the Poe laughter, go down the stairs and through the door.

The Bow Room has a Stalfos and a central pit — fall in and you're back at the earlier two-Stalfos room. Beat the first and a floating platform descends to fill the pit; two more appear, and if the second isn't killed fast enough the other revives! When all three are down, the large chest yields the Fairy Bow. Yippee!
Onward needs keys we don't have — remember the silver eye switches in the block room? At its very top (past the two Blue Bubbles), shoot the door's silver eye switch with the Bow.
Through the door: the twisted hallway has straightened, opening a new path. Enter the small room from earlier at its new angle and jump down to the large chest — the Boss Key!
You could climb back up the ladder, but something waits in the hole below: jump down, defeat the two Blue Bubbles, and the nearby door opens.

keywords: Optional: Gold Skulltula #53 from the pillars
steps.detail: Back in the northwest ruins: jump out onto the narrow pillar platforms with the recovery hearts, look up against the wall, and kill the Skulltula — Hookshot the token.
A Big Deku Baba blocks the way — arrows if you're lazy, or stun it with the Deku Nuts they tend to drop. Behind it, check the last alcove on the right for a door hiding in the shadows.

Inside: a Floormaster, the Wallmaster's nastier cousin — kill it fast. Z-target, shield in close, and right after it attacks, run up and slash; charge a spin attack while it's "dying" to catch all three mini-Floormasters at once! The small chest that drops holds an ever-elusive Small Key. Exit.

Take the door on the right for a shortcut back to the block room and scale it as before. Optional chest: on the Blue Block's level, an eye switch in the wide-open area — arrow it and jump over for more arrows. Then scale to the very top and shoot the silver eye switch again — yes, the eye is already closed; much in this game can be "done again" to reverse it. The hallway re-twists.

Back through the twisted corridor and the funky room, across the two platforms to the right-hand door: Joelle's room, the giggling picture frames. Approach a portrait with the ghost and she flees to another — so shoot her portrait from afar with the Fairy Bow (easiest from the middle level), using elimination across all three frames. She then materializes at the room's lowest level: fight her like the Graveyard Poes — shield up, sword or Bow when her guard drops, a Deku Nut to force her visible when she vanishes. Five good hits and she bursts into flame, relighting the red torches. Grab her arrows, open the chest for a Small Key, and go through the door.

Cross the Bow Room (nothing left there) into Beth's room — the second Poe Sister. Shoot her pictures and beat her in the bottom half of the room like her sister; the blue torches light. Then take the locked door at the top of the room.

Another funky-angled room, another Wallmaster warning from Navi — nothing to do here; jump across, or drop down and climb the ladder to the right-hand area. The hallway beyond has Green Bubbles — like the blue ones but circling fixed paths, occasionally flameless; pretty worthless as obstacles. Walk around them and use your last Small Key on the far door.

Lava Room — the goal is the silver eye switch on the far platform: hop onto a moving middle platform and either shoot an arrow through the central torch from the opposite side (a fire arrow, effectively), or jump to the switch's own platform and use Din's Fire. The hallway you came from twists; go back and drop into the hole that appears.

The long checkerboard room is easy, but the camera angle changes: no Z-targeting, and ranged weapons free-aim while you move. Wait for the ceiling to fall to read the safe path — shadowed squares hide drop-down Skulltulas; exploit the angle by holding the Hookshot button and releasing beside a Skulltula. Reach the switch to unlock the far door.

Amy's room: stand clear of the floor shadows and shoot the giant picture of Amy — five blocks fall, and you have one minute ten to assemble them. Mess up or time out? Leave and re-enter to reset (the first layout is the easiest). Trick: dump the blue middle block first, then push/pull the rest toward one chosen anchor block you never move — it saves time. With the four blocks aligned, Amy appears: beat her like her sisters. The flame lights and the door opens.

keywords: Beat Meg: shoot the one that spins twice
steps.detail: Kill the hallway Skulltula and return to the Grand Hall; jump down to find the last Poe Sister, Meg, chuckling (crying?) in the middle. She splits into four illusions — find the real one fast or she smacks you for half a heart. Spin Attacks, Din's Fire and Deku Nuts hit all four but she's invulnerable to them; instead, stand still and watch: the REAL one spins around one extra time. Z-target her and shoot with the Bow. Five hits and Meg returns to flame — the elevator at the temple's start reactivates. Restock from the nearby pots and ride it down.

keywords: Gold Skulltula #54 in the chest room
steps.detail: In the basement, push the big wall handles to spin the room and expose alcoves, opening the way to the Boss Room. Turn counter-clockwise once per stage: (1) press the switch on the white ground; (2) press the switch on the red ground; (3) open the chest and defeat the Gold Skulltula in that alcove; (4) press the switch on the blue ground. The final hallway opens — stock up on arrows, fairies and health before walking up the ramp.

keywords: Shoot the real rider ×3, then rally the orbs
steps.detail: The arena is empty — until you turn to leave. Ganondorf! Or… almost. Phase 1 plays like Meg: he emerges from one of six wall paintings with an illusion — the fake turns back at the last second while the real one charges out and zaps the arena's middle for two hearts if you're slow. Stay to one side rather than the middle (his lightning NEVER hits the corners) and shoot the real rider with the Bow; after three hits his horse abandons him. Phase 2: he floats and lobs energy orbs — bat them back with your sword in a game of ping-pong (time swings so the sword TIP meets the orb) until one connects and stuns him, then slash away. Rarely he telegraphs fireworks and charges fast: shield or sidestep, or eat three full hearts. About thirteen hits total and he falls; the real Ganondorf's voice dismisses his creation and banishes it to another dimension. Encouraging.

missable.deadline: During this fight — Drunken Master requires lowering Phantom Ganon to 1 HP using only Bow and Bottles; Dodgeball requires no healing with 3 hearts only

steps.detail: Snag the Heart Container and step into the blue light.

In the Chamber of Sages, Saria — the Sage of Forest — makes insinuations about mutual feelings and coughs up the Forest Medallion (sad, really; she seems trapped there for everyone else's good). You reappear in Kokiri beside the dead Deku Tree, where a wiggling stub of branch abruptly springs into a small chubby tree: the Deku Tree Sprout. He tells of Link's past — a woman wounded in a terrible war entrusted her child to the Great Deku Tree before dying, and the destined boy was raised as a Kokiri. You've grown because you're actually a Hylian; your Kokiri friends stay children forever.

keywords: Learn the Prelude of Light from Sheik
steps.detail: Navi suggests returning to the Temple of Time, where Sheik waits: she teaches the Prelude of Light, a warp straight back to the Temple of Time, and mentions you can replace the sword in the pedestal to become a child again. (Some goodies await back in childhood — handled later. On 3DS, talking to Sheik also unlocks the Boss Gauntlet: sleep in your old bed to refight beaten bosses.)

keywords: Adult Shooting Gallery: hit all targets
steps.detail: With the Bow, a nifty upgrade waits in Kakariko: the brand-new building just beyond the Skulltula House is the adult Shooting Gallery, virtually identical to the childhood one but played with Bow and Arrow. Hit all the targets for an upgraded Quiver!

Following the guide, you're past 50 Gold Skulltulas: the Skulltula House's newest prize is a piece of heart. (The final 100-token reward is just an infinite supply of Rupees — hardly worth it except for 100% completion.)

keywords: Optional: meet the Poe Shop owner
steps.detail: The last bottle is available now — time-consuming and only really worth it for 100% completion; you need the Fairy Bow and Epona, both acquired this chapter. The old guardhouse on the way out of ruined Hyrule Town is now the Poe Shop: the gangly one-eyed hooded figure loves Poes, buys small ones in bottles for a few Rupees — and offers a prize for catching all ten BIG ones scattered across Hyrule Field.

Big Poes appear only when you cross certain invisible trigger rings on the ground. Ride through them on Epona, whip out the Fairy Bow and shoot the Poe as it flees — two hits down it. Speak with a downed Poe to bottle it, then ferry them back to the Poe Shop repeatedly, as obnoxious as that is. Each turned-in Big Poe pays 50 Rupees.

#1: the sign right at the entrance to the ruined bridge toward what remains of Hyrule Town — passing it spawns the Poe. #2: the tree at the Lon Lon Ranch entrance — pass between it and the wall. #3: northwest (top-left) corner of the Field, among the little black bushes by the stream — the trigger is atop one of them. #4: the solitary tree between Lon Lon Ranch and the Gerudo Valley path — face Hyrule Castle and run along the tree's right side. #5: near the Gerudo Valley entrance where the road forks, on the ground between road and wall (right side) — face right and coax him through the hole there; by far the most frustrating one.

#6: south Field between Lake Hylia and Kokiri Forest, among the many trees — run through the patch of bushes. #7: same area, a lone brown boulder — the trigger is the tree directly south of it; face Lon Lon Ranch and run north along the tree's right side. #8: along the road between Kokiri Forest and Lon Lon Ranch sits a silver boulder — stand at the top, inside the road's "Y" next to it. #9: the "V"-shaped wall west of Lon Lon Ranch — wait in the corner, on top or running alongside below. #10: near the Kakariko entrance, the overhang above the stream — the general area triggers it; wait on top or run past the supporting pillar along the stream (up top is easier).

keywords: Optional: race the Running Man on Epona
steps.detail: While you're roaming Hyrule Field on Epona, take on the Running Man challenge for Wind Runner: summon Epona first, then start the race — mounted, it's an easy win in under 1 minute 30 seconds.

Each Big Poe nets 50 Rupees — pretty much worthless at this point; there's little to save for beyond quiver refills or replacing tunics eaten by Like Likes. But after all ten are turned in, the Ghost Shop owner rambles ecstatically and hands over the long-awaited final bottle. Doesn't it feel good to have them all?

keywords: Activate the Scarecrow's Song with Bonooru
steps.detail: (This whole section is optional goodies; skip ahead for the main quest.) If you made up a song for the lower scarecrow as a child (chapter 3), return to him as an adult between the Lakeside Laboratory and the Fishing Pond and play it back: the Scarecrow's Song activates. From now on, wherever Navi flies to the middle of nowhere and turns green, the Song of Time may summon a Time Block — or the Scarecrow's Song summons Pierre, a Hookshot target.

keywords: Optional: Heart Piece #23 on the Lab roof
steps.detail: Just outside the Lakeside Laboratory, get on top of the building — ride the grown Magic Bean plant, or play the Scarecrow's Song and Hookshot to Pierre. Climb the ladder to the heart piece.

The lake is drained, so the Fishing Pond can't be reached on foot — use the bean plant or the Scarecrow's Song. Inside, 20 Rupees buys the pole; adult fishing plays like childhood fishing with bigger fish. The largest is always right in the center: stand on the log near the middle, cast, and once hooked NEVER release A — hold down-left on the stick and grind it out; if it escapes, leave and re-enter. And DON'T SCARE THE FISH, or they bolt to the deep. Show the man a fish of thirteen pounds or more to win the Golden Scale.

keywords: Optional: hook the pond owner's hat
steps.detail: For Mischievous Hero: stand in front of the Fishing Pond owner and keep casting until your fishing rod grabs his hat, then cast the hat out into the pond to make it permanently disappear. Tricky to pull off, but there's no time limit.

keywords: Optional: catch the Hylian Loach (Sinking Lure)
steps.detail: The pond's giant Hylian Loach is a pure challenge — no real reward. It doesn't always spawn: pay, look, and if absent leave and retry (the Sun's Song to skip a day helps). It sits at the very bottom, unreachable without the Sinking Lure — hidden invisible at one of four spots, most often by the narrow stream; walk the area until found. With the lure: cast, press nothing and it sinks (if a wrong fish bites, keep pressing nothing until it breaks free). Land it near the Loach's mouth and wait; it rarely moves, but when it does it may bite — instantly hold A, then down-left on the stick, and never let go through all its wiggling. Show the Fisherman: 50 Rupees, and he releases it back due to its rarity.

keywords: Optional: Golden Scale dive in the Lab
steps.detail: Inside the Lakeside Laboratory, jump into the small pool and dive all the way down — with the Gold Scale you can touch the bottom of the tank, and the scientist takes note. Climb out and talk to him for a heart piece.

keywords: Optional: grotto NW of Lon Lon
steps.detail: At the lone tree northwest of Lon Lon Ranch, the Shard of Agony chimes: bomb open the hidden grotto, jump into the water below and dive to the bottom of the pond for a piece of heart.

keywords: Optional: bean plant at Dodongo's Cavern
steps.detail: En route to Goron City: at the Dodongo's Cavern entrance, if you planted a Magic Bean in the soft soil, ride the plant up to the ledge and jump off for the Heart Piece (the one the walkthrough skipped as a child — this is the intended way). Then enter the cavern.

Two tokens wait in Dodongo's Cavern. The first, in the giant staircase room, was collectable with the Boomerang as a child (chapter 5 covered it) — if you missed it, take the long route back (pillar lift to the second floor, around and down, across the large bridge, through the Armos room switch) and grab it from the cubby at the top of the staircase; the Hookshot works fine as an adult.

The second is adult-only: from the main room's first floor, take the door on the right (southeast) into the skinny Baby Dodongo room. Along the left wall, Navi flies up to a higher alcove and turns green — play the Scarecrow's Song, Hookshot to Pierre, and snag your fifty-fifth token.

keywords: Optional: night — Gold Skulltula #56 behind the pedestal
steps.detail: Make it night before entering Goron City. Walk across the tightropes straight ahead: the Gold Skulltula sits on the backside of the center pedestal at the top of the City.

The heart of the mountain is too hot to enter yet, so detour into the lonely City — only two Gorons remain, one rolling around the mid level like old times. Time a Bomb to blow him up: he yells that your name is his own — he's Darunia's son! He makes you listen to two little stories: Ganondorf awakened a terrible dragon in the Fire Temple and plans to feed the Gorons to it. After both tales he awards the Goron Tunic. Yay!

keywords: Pull the statue in Darunia's room
steps.detail: He reopens the shop and Darunia's room, and mentions a secret passage into the Crater: jump down, enter Darunia's Room and pull back the statue to reveal the way.

Enter the Crater from Darunia's room, Hookshot across the gap of the broken bridge, and walk along it until Sheik interrupts — with, yet again, another song: the Bolero of Fire, which warps you to Death Mountain Crater whenever you like. The song's rather boring, but those cinematic camera angles on steroids… Oooo.

keywords: Optional: child trip — bugs on the crater soil
steps.detail: Some collecting first: play the Prelude of Light to the Temple of Time, replace the sword in the Pedestal to become a child, bottle some bugs, and return here with the Bolero of Fire. Drop the bugs on the soft soil next to the Triforce platform for a Gold Skulltula, and — importantly — plant a Magic Bean before teleporting back and returning as an adult.

keywords: Optional: bean plant ride — Heart Piece #27
steps.detail: As an adult, ride the Magic Bean plant to the top of one of the giant volcano-like mountains — a Heart Piece sits atop one. (Also: the cubby across the lava holds the Heart Piece we climbed down to as a child; if you skipped it, the bean plant reaches that too.)

keywords: Bolero down, ladder to the Fire Temple
steps.detail: Goodies collected, play the Bolero of Fire to drop back to the warp pad, go straight forward and climb down the ladder to the Fire Temple entrance.

Run up the stairs (ignore the Fire Keese) and enter the left door. Darunia stands by the Boss Door: he repeats what his son told you, admits he couldn't find the "legendary hammer", and heads in to face the dragon without it — without even the Boss Key. Cheater!

Jump to the small platforms on the left, then the corner area; step on the switch to open the nearby cell and free the first Goron — several are caged through the temple, all with useful hints. This one: "do something" to the pillar in this room's ceiling (much later). Snag the key from the chest, return, and take the locked door on the opposite side.

The Lava Cavern is a big room of islands and floating platforms — with the Goron Tunic the lava only hurts slowly, so don't fret. The door across the bridge needs Small Keys from BOTH sides of the room. Start left (by the Time Block): work over and enter the ledge door. Free the Goron (hint: bombable walls sound different when slashed with the sword) and take the key.

Back on the platform, turn around and play the Song of Time where Navi turns green to bring the Time Block down, opening a higher door. Inside the small tile room, shield off the flying Tiles from one side, then kill the Like Like carefully (arrows recommended — it eats tunics and shields). The Gold Skulltula waits on the back wall. Return afterwards.

Cross to the room's opposite side, dodging Fire Keese — you can even walk on lava briefly without damage. The funny door-shaped wall there rings false when slashed (the Goron's clue is genuine): bomb it, free the third Goron inside (hint: Bombs can activate switches), take the key, then return to the bridge side and open the locked door.

keywords: Ride the block through the roof
steps.detail: Flaming Elevator room: get atop the metal fencing (slide down the wooden ramp and climb, or Hookshot it directly; if you fall in the lava, wait for the fire tick then Hookshot out quickly). Up top, kill the Keese on the two horizontal strips, then push the block on the right over the flaming pit. Jump onto the block and let the fire push it up through the roof into a new room. Go through the locked door.

keywords: Torch Slugs, block on the symbol
Bomb the crystal switch, climb the fence
steps.detail: A caged Goron sits ahead — unreachable from here; ignore him. Climb the left ledge and kill the Torch Slug (easy, but relentless once it sees you), then its buddy up the next ledge. Across the way, jump to the crystal switch beside a block: push the block off the ledge, then pull it until it fully covers the floor symbol, and use it to reach the platform's higher tiers. The top fencing is ringed with fire; the crystal switch pauses it too briefly to climb — so stand on the middle tier, drop a Bomb onto the switch, and climb to the top while it detonates below. Plenty of time now: up the fence and through the door.

keywords: Boulder maze: right wall to the switch
steps.detail: The crescent-shaped Boulder Maze is patrolled by boulders on illogical set paths; all the goodies hug the outer wall under ceiling overhangs. Follow the outer wall right to a floor switch: step on it, meet the fourth Goron (hint: the Door Mimics ahead die to a Bomb), and take the Small Key from the chest.

Back along the outer wall, past the face where you started, to a small alcove where the ceiling forms a triangle: the wall ahead looks normal but slashing it makes a "zipt" sound — and you can hear the Gold Skulltula behind it. Bomb it (northeast portion of the room) and claim the token.

Follow the wall again, two rights, to another door and a small dark corridor: free the Goron (hint: slash your sword or roll to put yourself out if you catch fire) and take the Small Key. Done here — continue counter-clockwise along the outer wall to the locked door.

In the small narrow-bridge room, watch your step — a fall drops you all the way back to the lava cavern. Shoot the silver eye switch above to open the right-hand door: the large chest inside holds the Dungeon Map. Return and take the locked door.

Flaming Wall of Death… Room… — walk the narrow stone and jump onto the ceiling-suspended metal grating. A wall of flame appears behind you and creeps forward; a hit knocks you down but resets it back a little. The real annoyance is the two Red Bubbles popping out of the lava at the "convenient" jump spots — you can even hop into the lava deliberately (it doesn't hurt instantly) to skirt them and jump back on. Follow the grating to the end, climb up, and go through the door.

Boulder Maze, upper level: platform-hop ahead to the Torch Slug's platform and kill it — Navi yells about a Goron below. There's a crack in the ground: bomb it open, climb down the grating, and the twisting hallway leads back to the earlier green room. The switch here opens another cell AND a massive shortcut. The freed Goron's hint: use Bombs against the Flare Dancer later. Snag the key and head back up.

A nearby platform holds a switch, reached by a "U-turn" across the narrow platforms. Activating it opens a cage high on the other side of the room. Work over there — Hookshot the Torch Slug on the small platform first to stun it so you can't be knocked off — then jump across, kill it, and reach the Goron (hint: play your Ocarina when somewhere on the map seems unreachable — that's next). He then walks off on mid-air. I wish I could do that… Take the small key and return to the middle of the room.

(Optional detour; the main path continues at the flaming-wall room.) By the upper maze entrance near that first Torch Slug and the bombed hole: face the room from the door, turn right, and Navi marks a platform above. Play the Scarecrow's Song, Hookshot to Pierre, then turn and Hookshot to the small elevator platform that carries you up. Go through the door.

Secret Room 1: round the corner and climb the grate to a Gold Skulltula; take its token and climb to the next door. Secret Room 2: forward a little, then immediately left — another Gold Skulltula on the wall; Hookshot the token.

A nearby switch douses the flames around the chest on the ledge above — just a gold Rupee (200), not really worth it, but if you want the challenge: Hookshot to the target atop the steep ledge, then sprint the rest, cutting close to the edge; you barely make it. To leave, jump into the center of the pit and fall all the way to the small circular room with the narrow bridges, then through the door to the flaming-wall room.

Objective: the next locked door (you should hold two Small Keys). In the flaming-wall room, halfway through, a ledge with a locked door sits along the outer wall: reach a high point of the grating, jump to it, go through, and continue down the small hallway.

The big "C"-shaped room hides invisible fire walls between its skinny pillars (visible only up close), rolling boulders, and fake doors that fall on you — spot them by how they stick OUT from the wall instead of being indented; a Bomb disposes of them. Head right along the outer wall, kill the Fire Keese, dodge the boulder, and mind the fire in the pillars right before the door ahead — walk around it. The small room holds the Compass chest. That's all this side offers.

keywords: Left side to the last locked door
steps.detail: Work back to the room's left side — the "door" across the way is fake. Past the giant pillar and the two spinning fire-spitting faces lies another invisible-wall maze: stay to the inner side, move slowly (no boulders here, just one Fire Keese), and spend your last Small Key on the locked door. =(

keywords: Note the trapped Goron + Time Block shortcut
steps.detail: A trapped Goron sits in this room, but nothing can be done for him yet. Playing the Song of Time where Navi glows green drops a Time Block from the ledge above — a shortcut to the upper level, not important right now. Continue through the next door.

keywords: Switch, sprint past the flame wall
steps.detail: Back on the maze's far side, past the invisible fire wall (a Fire Keese wants your nice red hat). The door on the left hides behind a wall of flaming death: the switch across from you kills it briefly, but invisible walls block the direct line — scout your path around the flaming pillar to the right BEFORE pressing, then run it fast. Safely across, loot the jars; the door in the cubby is another Door Mimic — Bomb it to reveal the hidden door behind.

keywords: Bomb/Hookshot the body, chase the torso
steps.detail: The Flare Dancer: easy once understood, awkward if you stall. It leaps from the flames spinning — stop it fast or it sprays flames (red ones sit; blue ones creep around the room); otherwise it skates around leaving a fire trail, knocking you over harmlessly but annoyingly. To hurt it: as it holds still before attacking, target it and throw a Bomb or Hookshot it — the flaming body strips away, leaving a squeaking black torso on tiny legs that circles the room and reverses when it sees you. Run the OPPOSITE direction, collide, and as it turns, nail a Jump Attack and slash away; repeat. Its color tracks its health like the Torch Slugs — red to blue to green. Hookshot-strip then circle-and-Jump-Attack is the fastest kill. The flames on the platform die with it and the door reopens: climb atop the platform to ride up a level.
In the next small hallway, climb the side grating, drop a Bomb over the edge onto the crystal switch (the earlier trick), climb up and enter the door.

Hammer Cavern — difficult AND annoying: step on the ledge switch, then run the skinny platform up to the giant chest before the fire returns; fall off and you redo the whole climb back to this room. Tip: cast Farore's Wind at the door first for a warp back up (optional spell — not required). Clear the Fire Keese along the left side up to the chest first so they can't bother the run, jump back to the switch, and go. A few tries maybe, but the prize is the Megaton Hammer.
Jump to the lowest level and Hammer the small floor platform bearing the Fire Temple's symbol. Drop through the revealed hole and Hammer down the totem-like statue heads blocking a door. Go through.

"L"-shaped room: run forward, turn and kill the Keese first, then smash the oddly placed pillar with the Hammer — the floor collapses into giant steps leading down to a door. Before descending, pick up one of the nearby boxes (re-enter the room if you broke them) and carry it down: place it on the switch at the bottom to hold the door open, and go through.

keywords: Smash the platform, then the rusty switch
steps.detail: The next tiny room is obvious: smash the lone platform (standing on top of it is fun) to drop back into the giant crescent room. Climb onto the platform, admire the rusty switch you may have noticed earlier — and Hammer it. The long-closed door opens.

keywords: Rusty switch frees Goron #8, Small Key
steps.detail: In the small Time Block room, play the Song of Time to move the block if you haven't — another upper-level shortcut — revealing a rusty switch across the way. Jump over, Hammer it, and the Goron below is freed: his hint points at a statue in the starting room. Snag the Small Key, climb back up via the Time Block, and go through the door.

keywords: It crashes down to the entrance
steps.detail: The first Goron's hint pays off: in the main invisible-fire-wall room, get on top of the giant platform that runs straight through the floor and swing the Hammer. Fall through the hole — you ride it all the way back to the room where you met Darunia, creating the platform path to the Boss Room! No Boss Key yet, though… Leave the room.

In the entrance room, find the statue the last Goron mentioned: stand at its left or right side and pound repeatedly with the Hammer, then take the locked door revealed. The small hallway beyond teems with Fire Keese and Torch Slugs: sword the Keese first, then flip the Torch Slugs harmlessly over with the Hammer and finish them.
More flying Tiles and a Like Like: block the Tiles, kill everything, and take the Gold Skulltula from the back wall. Head through the door on the left.

keywords: Then the rusty switch: Boss Key!
steps.detail: Another Flare Dancer — much easier with the Megaton Hammer: smash the ground to strip its flaming body, chase it down (or Hookshot) and slash. The chest it leaves holds Bombs. Through the right-hand door, smash the rusty switch in the small hallway and speak with the Goron for confirmation that it is indeed "Boss Time": the giant chest holds the Boss Key. Leave through the last door.

keywords: Bottle the fairy, enter the boss door
steps.detail: Back at the entrance room, the pillar you hammered should be shoved into the lava, forming the platform across to the boss door (if not, it awaits your Hammer up in the crescent room). Before entering: a Fairy hides in a jar on the ledge to the right, if you want one bottled.

keywords: Hammer the head when it leans out
steps.detail: The platform behind you sinks into the lava — trapped. Volvagia bursts from the central pit: a worm-like dragon circling a hexagonal platform ringed by lava (falling in is fine — climb back up; you can even hang off the side on purpose to dodge attacks). Its three attacks all hurt more than previous bosses: (1) it bumps the ceiling to rain flaming rocks — they target where you stood when it flew up, so relocate and run from shadows; (2) it flies the arena breathing fire — stand next to the hole it emerged from (its turn radius is too wide to reach you) or circle/shield; (3) it leans its head out of a hole and pauses before breathing fire — YOUR moment: run up and Hammer its head, then slash away at the stun. Arrows also chip it while it flies (they can't land the final blow). As it weakens it fakes you out among the nine lava holes — two fakes in a row, then the third is real; stand mid-arena and run to the last one (lava-walking briefly is fine now). Several cycles and it grabs its head, screams, and burns from the inside out, its skeleton disintegrating as it falls.

missable.deadline: During this fight — Whac-A-Mole requires only the Megaton Hammer, no healing, 3 hearts only; Hot Hot Hot requires winning without wearing the Goron Tunic

steps.detail: The triumphant music announces the blue teleport light — but snag the Heart Container first!

The land around Death Mountain is cleansed with the dragon dead. In the Chamber of Sages, Darunia — the obvious Sage of Fire — beams about how proud he is and how you've become "a real man", then hands over the Fire Medallion. You reappear on the Crater's teleport pad.

With the Hammer, nearby goodies open up. Head left back across the bridge (Hookshot the broken part and climb up), then work as far left as possible, over a bridge to three red boulders — Bombs don't crack these; Hammer the two on the right blocking the entrance. Inside, play Zelda's Lullaby on the Triforce symbol: the Great Fairy of Wisdom doubles your Magic Meter. Nifty. Exit and smash the red boulder on your right for the fastest way out via the Crater entrance to Death Mountain Trail.

Red boulders everywhere can now be broken — two Gold Skulltulas are quick grabs. First, in the valley at the top of the mountain where flaming boulders rained on you as a child (they've stopped — Volvagia is dead): make it night and smash the red boulder closest to the summit; the Skulltula hides behind it.

keywords: Optional: night — Gold Skulltula #64 (above Dodongo's Cavern)
steps.detail: Second: by the Goron City entrance, on the ledge above Dodongo's Cavern where you tossed the Bomb Flower in the past, sits a red boulder — another Gold Skulltula beneath it at night; the Megaton Hammer breaks it.

keywords: Note the bean plant to the Domain
steps.detail: Next destination: Zora's River. If you're nowhere near it, play the Prelude of Light to the Temple of Time, then out to Hyrule Field (call Epona if you wish); Zora's River is in the east, where the water starts. Rounding the corner past the old Bean Salesman's spot, a Magic Bean plant awaits if you planted a bean there as a child — its path takes you most of the way to Zora's Domain. Two Skulltulas to grab here first.

keywords: Optional: night — Gold Skulltula #65 (south wall plateau)
steps.detail: The first is tricky, high on the south wall in the middle of the area — you need to get on the giant central plateau. Ride the bean plant and jump off onto the platform as soon as you can, or (if you never planted it) follow the path until it narrows near Zora's Domain, turn right down the skinny water back toward the entrance, and climb the ladder on the ledge. At night, Hookshot the token.

keywords: Optional: Gold Skulltula #66 (jump the fence, Hookshot)
steps.detail: Heading on toward Zora's Domain, once you cross the small river, look high on the wall for a Gold Skulltula just out of reach from the ground. Jump onto the fence and the Hookshot reaches it — snag the token.

keywords: Play Zelda's Lullaby at the waterfall
steps.detail: Go up to the grating in front of the waterfall and play Zelda's Lullaby to part the red seas. Jump in, Moses!

keywords: Optional: night — Gold Skulltula #67 by the frozen waterfall
steps.detail: Eep! The Domain is frozen and the Zoras are gone. Nothing quest-wise here, but one more Gold Skulltula: at night, look up near the top of the frozen waterfall (Sun's Song if needed). Go up through King Zora's chamber, walk right up to the edge, turn left and Hookshot the token.
Back at King Zora, the poor guy is trapped in a red, transparent shield-like Red Ice (doesn't look very ice-like). He's stuck for now — go on through to Zora's Fountain.

Outside it's frozen too, and Lord Jabu-Jabu is gone — who knows what happened to him. Step onto Jabu-Jabu's altar and jump across the floating icebergs (climb back onto non-spinning platforms if you fall; dodge the Octoroks — don't bother killing them, just keep moving). A Heart Piece sits alone off to the right on one of the ice platforms: wait for the small spinning platforms to come your way and jump, accounting for the slippery ice.

keywords: Cross the spinning platforms to the cavern
steps.detail: Turn around and head the opposite way, toward the cavern in the wall across the water (it was there in the past, unreachable then). Cross the three spinning platforms in a row — jump normally; their spin only matters if you go slow — and the final one that holds still. A running start grabs the ledge to pull up.

First Hallway — slash any stalagmites blocking your path, but keep moving past the lighter-colored stalactites: they fall the moment you pass under, so move when you hear their "icy noises". Down the twisting hallway, ignore the purposeless chunk of Red Ice.

At the hallway's end, a large room of Freezards, which freeze you if you're careless. Target them from afar, wait for them all to attack at once, then Hookshot the nearest (chipping it) and kill it — usually two before retreating; or circle so they attack empty space and hit them from behind. A Blade Trap circles the room, plus one invisible one that appears, strikes and vanishes — watch for it. Easiest: stand in the middle and use Din's Fire. With all four dead, the icy bars lift; go through the passage flanked by two Red Ice.
Watch for the stalactite waiting on the ceiling right before the stalagmites. The jars here hold Recovery Hearts.

The giant room holds a spinning ice blade of death (of course) — collect five Silver Rupees to open the next door. Dodge the blade by running counter-clockwise with it, or duck by holding R (rolling works but is trickier); shield off the floating Jars. There's a Gold Skulltula high on the wall just above the first Silver Rupee — Hookshot it. Gather all five Rupees and the bars on the ledge above move aside.

keywords: Map Room: clear Ice Keese + Freezard
steps.detail: Ice Keese here freeze you for a long time on contact — a deadly combo with the Freezards, so first wander the room clearing the lone ground Freezard and any Ice Keese. Climb onto the entry platform and kill the Freezard at the far end (target from below and Hookshot up as it stops blowing), then jump the tiny platforms left. Navi flags the strange Blue Fire — the dungeon's key trick: Blue Fire melts Red Ice. Empty most of your bottles and fill them with Blue Fire (all four if you've followed along — you only need one, but more is better). Kill the staring Freezard, round the corner to the large chest encased in Red Ice, melt it with Blue Fire, and open it for the Dungeon Map. Refill Blue Fire and head back to the Spinning Blade room.

keywords: Compass Cavern: Compass + Heart Piece #29 + Gold Skulltula #69
steps.detail: Back in the main room, two side paths are blocked by Red Ice — go left first, melting the ice. The short hallway opens into the Compass Cavern: Keese line the walls and the ceiling stalactites reappear shortly after falling, so slash forward to the Blue Fire, kill the Keese atop it and refill. Then the goodies: the Compass in a frozen chest on the right; on the other side a Heart Piece frozen in Red Ice (melt with Blue Fire) and a Gold Skulltula high on a pillar (Hookshot). Refill your bottles and return to the main room.

keywords: Ice Block Cavern: kill Ice Keese, Gold Skulltula #70
steps.detail: This time melt the Red Ice at the west exit on the opposite side of the room. Through the "S"-shaped hallway you emerge in a slippery blue room of square checkered ice: circle it killing all the Ice Keese, then grab the Gold Skulltula waiting on the wall (if you don't see it… I feel sorry for you…).

keywords: Push the ice block for the Silver Rupees
steps.detail: Collect all the Silver Rupees by pushing the Ice Block around and climbing it (push it into a pit to reset it to start). More Blue Fire waits across the room; the floating red Rupees overhead are reachable with the Song of Time but pointless. With Blue Fire: push the block into the room, then right toward the north wall — an alcove holds a Silver Rupee in Red Ice (melt it), then drop the block into the abyss. From the start position, push it left, climb up and onto the small pillar for the second Rupee, then keep pushing right, collecting Rupees and grabbing Blue Fire on the way (play the Song of Time where Navi flies over to make time blocks up to the far Blue Fire). Keep at least two bottles of Blue Fire. Finally push the block in front of the newly opened hallway, climb atop it and in.
Work through the final hallway watching the two Freezards, and at the end melt the wall of Red Ice with a bottle of Blue Fire. Go through the door.
In the small disco-like room, a White Wolfos (the game's only one) fights just like regular Wolfos — an easy battle. The large chest that appears holds the Iron Boots. Nifty.

keywords: Learn the Serenade of Water from Sheik
steps.detail: Sheik appears with more poetry: the Zoras were trapped under the ice, and she freed only the Zora princess (Ruto), who went to the Water Temple. She teaches the Serenade of Water, warping you to Lake Hylia.

keywords: Iron Boots test: sink and open the door
steps.detail: Try the Iron Boots: open the Equipment screen and press A to equip them (annoying, isn't it?), then step into the water. Underwater you can open the door — beyond it a timer appears, since you can only stay under for a limited time. Climb out, re-equip the Kokiri Boots, jump down, and with at least one bottle of Blue Fire, head for the Ice Cavern's exit.

keywords: Optional: Iron Boots dive — Heart Piece #30
steps.detail: Be sure you carry at least one bottle of Blue Fire (return to the cavern for one if not). Jump into the water at Zora's Fountain and put on the Iron Boots to sink to the very bottom, where a Heart Piece rests on the ground floor.

keywords: Buy the Zora Tunic at the shop (300R)
steps.detail: Return to Zora's Domain. To set up the Kiss That Frog achievement, buy the Zora Tunic BEFORE unfreezing King Zora: melt the Red Ice blocking the Zora Shop below with Blue Fire, purchase the Zora Tunic for 300 Rupees, and equip it. The Zora Tunic lets you breathe underwater indefinitely. (The 30-Gold-Skulltula reward, the Giant's Wallet, helps afford this.)

keywords: Unfreeze King Zora while wearing the Zora Tunic
steps.detail: With the Zora Tunic equipped, use Blue Fire to unfreeze King Zora from the Red Ice, then stand on the platform below and speak with him — because you're wearing a Zora Tunic, he blows you a kiss (Kiss That Frog). Important: if you talk to him WITHOUT a Zora Tunic equipped, he simply gives you a free Zora Tunic instead and you miss the kiss for good.

missable.deadline: When you unfreeze and talk to King Zora — Kiss That Frog needs a Zora Tunic already equipped; talk to him without one and he hands over a free tunic instead of the kiss

steps.detail: A word on the Giant's Knife: Medigoron in Goron City's second level (behind bombable walls) sells adults a giant two-handed "knife" that shatters after a few swings and can be rebought endlessly. The unbreakable version — Biggoron's Sword — comes from a trading sequence, doable much earlier but slotted here to keep temple order. It's optional but the reward is sweet; you'll need Epona (chapter 7). Start with the Cucco Lady in Kakariko: she's breeding a new Cucco and gives you a Pocket Egg to test-run, since you look like a good Cucco raiser.

Hatch the egg by reaching dawn (Sun's Song twice, or wait in Hyrule Field), then enter the house right below the stairs (across from the Skulltula House and Shooting Gallery). Talon sleeps on the bed again — use the chicken to wake him and he returns to Lon Lon Ranch (if you already got Epona there). Return to the Cucco Lady, who gives you Cojiro.

keywords: Show Cojiro to the pale man
steps.detail: Cojiro belonged to the Cucco Lady's lost brother — the freaky pale guy you may have seen in Kakariko at night. In the Lost Woods, take the first left to find him; show him Cojiro and he asks for help, handing over the Odd Mushroom.

A three-minute timer now starts, and during timed trades you can't use a warp song or you fail. Leave the woods for Hyrule Field (faster if you planted the nearby magic bean), jump on Epona and ride to Kakariko Village.

Run to the last building on the right heading toward Death Mountain — the Potion Shop, open only by day (Sun's Song if night). Go through the back door on the left, appear outside, jump down and up the ramp to a little door: the old hag's secret shop, where she trades you the Odd Potion.

keywords: Give the Odd Potion to the Kokiri girl
steps.detail: Bring the Odd Potion back to the Lost Woods, to the Kokiri girl who tells you the Cucco Lady's brother has turned into a Stalfos — lovely. She demands the Odd Potion back (it belongs in the forest) and gives you the Poacher's Saw the man left behind.

Head to Gerudo Valley and cross the bridge (a running start on Epona or the Longshot gets you over if it isn't fixed). Speak with the fat guy here, who trades you the Broken Goron's Sword for the saw.

Take the broken sword to the very top of Death Mountain, just outside the Crater, to Biggoron (warp to the Crater with the Bolero of Fire since this trade isn't timed). Volcano dust has blinded him; he takes the broken sword and gives you the Prescription.
Go to Zora's Domain and speak with the now-unfrozen King Zora. He doesn't have the eye drops, only the ingredients, and trades you the Eyeball Frog.

keywords: 3-min timed: Eyeball Frog → World's Finest Eye Drops
steps.detail: Another three-minute timer: run out of Zora's Domain, jump into the stream, stay left and swim fast back to Hyrule Field, then gallop Epona southwest to Lake Hylia over the fences. Give the Eyeball Frog to the freaky old scientist in the Lakeside Laboratory — he's thrilled (thinks he can eat it) but turns it into the World's Finest Eye Drops.

The hard one: four minutes, but far tougher. Run out, ride Epona back to Kakariko, straight through and up the mountain (the bean planted at Dodongo's Cavern saves lots of time), across the valley that used to rain flaming rocks. Fairy-Bow the Skullwalltulas above and climb — you should make it with seconds to spare. Biggoron takes the drops and gives the Claim Check; the sword takes a couple of days.

Pass several days (play the Sun's Song a few times) and speak with Biggoron again to finally get the Biggoron's Sword. Whew! This two-handed blade does twice the Master Sword's damage but bars the shield — the weapon for the hack-first, ask-questions-later type.

keywords: Warp to Lake Hylia (Serenade of Water)
Optional: Gold Skulltula #71 in the Lab
steps.detail: With the Iron Boots and Zora Tunic, the Water Temple is open. Play the Serenade of Water to warp to Lake Hylia. One optional Gold Skulltula first: in the Lakeside Laboratory, use the Iron Boots to sink to the bottom of the small pool, roll into the wooden crate to pop out the Skulltula, and Hookshot the token.

keywords: Iron Boots + Hookshot the crystal to open it
steps.detail: The lake has drained, exposing the ruins — follow them to the lowest point and sink down to the grated temple entrance. To open it, wear the Iron Boots and Zora Tunic, stand right before it, and Hookshot the crystal above the doorway to lift the gate. Enter the temple.

You enter the large central chamber — the main room, which you'll return to often. Jump in and sink to the very bottom with the Iron Boots, then head through the opening on the bottom floor to the right, between the two torches (green-lined in the 3DS version); Hookshot the enemy Spikes on the way. Round the "S"-shaped corridor for a cutscene with — gasp — the adult Princess Ruto, all grown up! After an awkward one-way chat reminding you she's your fiancée, she floats up: take off your boots and follow her all the way to the top, where she's vanished. Head through the door.
Dungeon Map Room — four Spikes at once; kill them from afar to avoid damage, or Deku Nuts to stun them all up close. The large chest that appears holds the Dungeon Map.

keywords: Play Zelda's Lullaby at the first Triforce
steps.detail: Back in the previous room, stand before the Triforce symbol on the wall and play Zelda's Lullaby (the game's cue at every such symbol) — the water drops to its lowest level, the first of the three water-level spots Ruto mentioned. Drop to the lowest floor: the central torch there is now lit, so shoot arrows through it to light the other two, or use Din's Fire. Both lit, enter the small room.
The Shell Blades here can be Hookshot- or Bow-killed when their mouths are open, or all at once with Din's Fire up close. Take the Small Key from the chest and return to the main room.

keywords: Switch raises water, Hookshot the statue
steps.detail: Take the south entrance (directly to your left) to a dead end with a cracked floor: Bomb it open and sink down to a Shell Blade — Hookshot it or slip off your boots and swim over. Up the other side, get on the small platform and step on the switch to raise the water a little; the submerged statue rises, revealing a Hookshot target. Zip across and deal with the two Tektites (easier to slip past them first).

keywords: Open the gate (crystal switch / Spin Attack)
steps.detail: Open the gate — 3DS: slash the crystal switch in front of it; N64: Spin Attack at the gate to activate the switch beyond it. Snag the Gold Skulltula token and goodies, then return to the main room.

keywords: West: push the block into the hole
steps.detail: Through the west entrance a block bars the way: stand on the platform and push it all the way back until it drops into a hole and clicks in place underwater. Sink down and up the other side.

keywords: Water pillar room: crystal switch, ride up
steps.detail: First Water Pillar Room — cross to the other side (Hookshot back up if you fall in the pit): slash the crystal switch, jump onto the rising water pillar, hop across and run past the Tektite to the door.

keywords: Spinning Water Room: land on the dragon
steps.detail: The rapidly spinning pool sweeps you along if you're too light: with the Iron Boots, land on the underwater iron dragon statue that weaves through the walls, in the corner opposite the door. Standing still on it, Hookshot the crystal switch in the dragon's mouth to open the nearby door — only open a few seconds, so Hookshot the wall target in the alcove and run for it. Two Shell Blades make this annoying — best to just avoid them by slipping your boots off near the entrance to float up. Climb up, open the chest for a Small Key, hit the crystal switch here and swim back to the entrance (a Bomb buys time, but the sword works too) and go through the door.

keywords: Back through the pillar room to the main room
steps.detail: Back in the First Water Pillar Room, kill the Tektite and Hookshot the target across (or arrow the crystal switch), and work back to the main room — at the end, Iron Boots and Hookshot get you onto the platform.

keywords: Center pillar: Zelda's Lullaby at the 2nd Triforce
steps.detail: With two keys, enter the locked door in the center pillar (red-lined in 3DS — another water-level spot). Run forward, turn, and Hookshot up to the target on the platform above; climb up and play Zelda's Lullaby at the second Triforce to bring the water to mid level.

The part that stumps most people: the cutscene showed a platform below rising with the water, revealing a hidden hole. Stand atop either platform, equip the Iron Boots and step between them to land safely on the platform below, then sink into the hole.

keywords: Flooded basement: Hookshot the switch, clear the baddies
steps.detail: Round the corner to a lone crystal switch: Hookshot it from afar to open a gate that drops baddies down. Defeat them one at a time, inching forward, until the gate above on the far side opens. Float up for the Small Key in the chest, then work back to the water-level room and through the door.

With water at mid level, more rooms open. Optional Compass: enter the east wing with the two lone jars, leading to spikes. Hookshot the platform across from the floor spikes, turn and Hookshot the farthest ceiling target to rise all the way up. The crystal switch's timer is short, so lay a Bomb or arrow it from across to open the chest in time. Return to the main room, careful not to land on the spikes.

keywords: Ruto room: bomb the cracked wall
steps.detail: Sink to the lowest level and go back through the east entrance where you met Ruto; take off your boots and float up to a suspicious cracked wall all by itself. Bomb it and take the Small Key from the little chest. Back to the main room.

keywords: Lure the Tektite, ride the pillar up
steps.detail: With three keys, take the locked door on the west side (blue-lined in 3DS — another water-level spot). Another water pillar activated by the crystal switch across the room: lure a Tektite down near the pillar and kill it first, then stand on the pillar and Bomb or arrow the switch to go up.
Out the door you land in a main-room alcove beside the final Triforce symbol: play Zelda's Lullaby once more to lift the water back to its original highest height.

With two keys, enter the locked west door at the highest level: an enormous Waterfall Room. Kill the two Keese, drop to the lowest platform below (a Skulltula sits too far on the wall — later). Hookshot across to the falling platforms and work up to the opposing door — frustrating as you lose ground — then Hookshot onto the platform and enter the locked door.

Hookshot the central crystal switch to raise and lower the water repeatedly — raising it floats the statues up to reveal targets (fall in while low and you must Iron-Boot down and Hookshot the switch to get back up). Raise the water, lure the Tektites to the start platform and kill them. Then kill the two Blue Tektites up here and the Like Like across the spikes (Spin Attack or Bombs), Hookshot the ceiling target to reach the far side, and grab the jar goodies — a mini-boss is ahead.

keywords: Cross to the far door, spot Dark Link
steps.detail: One of the coolest rooms in the game: an endless illusory sea with ruins and a single tree, ringed by invisible metal-clanging walls. It seems empty — walk to the opposite door and turn around to see something by the tree in the middle.

Dark Link mirrors your every move at first, growing more aggressive as it goes. Easiest options if you have them: Din's Fire hits him every cast (limited by magic); the Biggoron's Sword demolishes him — just walk up and slash. With only the Master Sword it's trickier: targeting and tapping attack lets him block 3–4 times before a hit lands; swinging WITHOUT targeting almost always connects but leaves you exposed. Megaton Hammer jump attacks also work but leave you vulnerable. Beaten, the illusion ends, the room reverts, and the next door opens. Spooky.

keywords: Song of Time the block, drop down
steps.detail: Longshot Room — open the large chest for the Longshot! Stand by the Time Block on the far side of the chest and play the Song of Time to clear it, then fall into the cavern below.

keywords: Raging Water Cavern: swim the vortexes
steps.detail: This twisting corridor is full of rushing water and vortexes that reset the room if they suck you in. Swimming is generally better than the Iron Boots (the sloped ground makes you slide) — take it slow, press B to swim fast when needed. Jump in, hug the left wall past the first vortex. At the next corner, put the boots on and Longshot the Gold Skulltula on the wall, then take them off and round the corner hugging the right wall.

Ahead lie two vortexes — don't use the boots (you'll get pulled down); swim along the right wall to the little ledge with two jars and climb up to get your bearings. Swim across to the small platform opposite (jump in near the corner platform, wait until you float safely, then swim between the raging waters — or inch with the Iron Boots and pull them off to climb). Fairy-Bow the golden eye switch on the far wall to open the doorway for a few seconds, jump across and open the chest for a Small Key.

keywords: Back through the spinning room to the main room
steps.detail: Jump down into the spinning dragon room from earlier and work back to the main room the way you came.

keywords: Optional: Longshot the Waterfall Room Skulltula #74
steps.detail: The water should be at its highest. Before changing it, enter the west door back to the Waterfall Room with the descending platforms: now that you have the Longshot, easily snag the Skulltula on the right wall. Return to the main room.

Two more rooms open with the Longshot. Sink to the bottom, go through the east corridor where you met Ruto, float up and set the water to its lowest. Then enter the center pillar, Longshot up and play Zelda's Lullaby again; before exiting, Longshot the Skulltula token hiding at the top of the room.

Face the south corridor with the golden eye switch and iron grating (lure and kill the Blue Tektites first). Arrow the golden eye switch to lift the grating briefly — only enough time to Longshot in now. Down the corridor is a red block reachable earlier from another direction: pull it back until it locks, turn around and Longshot up to the target back to the main room.

Once more arrow the eye switch and Longshot into the south corridor. This time push the red block to reveal the opening on the right, run ahead and open the chest for the last Small Key, then Longshot up to the target back to the main room.

keywords: North corridor: Longshot past the spike wall
steps.detail: With two Small Keys, the second newly opened room is on the bottom level (no water change needed). Sink down and enter the north corridor (the sandy area with the ditch and two Spikes); sink and float up to a room with a spike wall blocking the far door. Turn around, climb onto the ledge, Longshot the target on the far side and enter the locked door.

Strong zig-zagging water with vortexes, six Blue Tektites and two rolling boulders. Either sneak past everything or lure the enemies to your side (safer). Swimming across is easy — stay on the right side near the end and swim under the Tektites, slipping on the Iron Boots if you drift toward a vortex. Across, enter the next door.

keywords: Stinger Room: bomb both south walls
Push the block onto the switch
steps.detail: Kill the annoying Stingers first. An underwater floor switch must be pressed down using a large block hidden behind a fake wall: both walls on the south end are odd — Bomb both cracked walls to reveal two passages, forming a "U"-shaped room with the block in the middle. Push and pull it out through much back-and-forth across the water until it drops onto the floor switch. Water raised, climb up and through the door.

keywords: Second Water Pillar Room: pillar timer opens the door
steps.detail: Same as the first pillar room but with more pillars and Tektites — lure them off first (jump close to the far side, or arrow them). Here the pillar timer also opens the door on the other side.

keywords: Optional: Gold Skulltula #76 from the boulder alcove
Kill the Shell Blade, last locked door
steps.detail: Now up in one of the boulder alcoves of the Rolling Boulder Room: inch to the edge and Longshot the token on your right. Wait for the boulder to pass, Iron Boots on, jump into the water toward the waterfall and sink into the deeper water. Round the corner slowly — a Shell Blade waits; Longshot it. Then take off the boots, float up, and use your last Small Key on the locked door.
Open the pretty chest for the Boss Key. Now backtrack all the way to the main room.

Raise the water to its highest level: on the second floor, take the west door that leads to the Triforce symbol and raise the floor. Back in the main chamber with the water high, Longshot onto the target of the northern dragon statue. Bottle Fairies from the nearby jars if you like, then enter the door.

keywords: Spike Trap Corridor: run straight to the boss door
steps.detail: Run past the Spike Traps staying to the left or right side and going straight — slow down or roll and you slide all the way down, likely getting hit. At the top, head through the menacing door.

keywords: Longshot the nucleus, corner it and slash
steps.detail: Navi notes the water isn't natural. Jump onto a middle platform to start. Morpha is a single orb that controls the water through grabbing tentacles — you can hear where each is coming from. Stay well clear as each tentacle appears; when the nucleus attacks through one, target it and Longshot to grab it, pulling it out where there's plenty of floor so it doesn't fall back into the water, then smack it with the sword. It escalates to two, then three tentacles at once — repeat; a tentacle that catches you squeezes and flings you. Fast strategy: from a corner, Longshot Morpha, then run around so Morpha is between you and the corner, hit it so it bounces into the corner, and pummel it there where it can't return to the water. Defeated, it bursts into water that sucks up to the ceiling and trickles away.

missable.deadline: During this fight — Day of the Tentacles requires beating Morpha without falling into the water or getting captured; Tentacles of the Deep adds no damage taken, with 3 hearts only

steps.detail: Grab the Heart Container before entering the glowing circle. In the Temple of Light, Ruto — rambling about your "engagement" and your search for Zelda — finally gives you the Water Medallion.

keywords: Shoot the sun from the pedestal
steps.detail: You reappear at Lake Hylia as it refills, with Sheik reciting more poetry before vanishing. The small pedestal on the island reads "shoot for the morning light" — it's automatically morning as the scene loads, so stand just in front, aim and shoot the sun with an arrow. A short clip reveals the Fire Arrows out on the small island; swim out and grab them.

keywords: Optional: night — Longshot the tree Gold Skulltula #77
steps.detail: Return to the island just above the Water Temple entrance, play the Sun's Song for night, and Longshot up onto the tree (aim at an angle to land on the branch). Walk the branch and look on top of the tree for a Gold Skulltula.

Entering Kakariko after the Water Temple, a scene plays: the village is in flames, and Sheik grimly faces the boss of the coming Shadow Temple as it emerges from the well where it was sealed (great place to store something evil — in the water supply). After you're beaten up, Sheik teaches the Nocturne of Shadow. Too bad we couldn't teleport to this town earlier! You could technically enter the Shadow Temple now, but a mini-dungeon first grants a very useful item.

keywords: Windmill: learn the Song of Storms from Guru-Guru
steps.detail: We first learned about the Song of Storms after the Hookshot but never needed it — get it now if you haven't. Enter the Windmill and speak with Guru-Guru, the furious guy at his music box, who rants about a "stupid kid" who came seven years ago and played "that song" that wrecked the windmill. Pull out your Ocarina and he teaches it to you. *snicker*

keywords: Return to childhood at the pedestal
steps.detail: The Bottom of the Well can only be entered as a child. Play the Prelude of Light to the Temple of Time and place the Master Sword in the pedestal to become Young Link again. With the Song of Storms now, a few optional goodies are collectable as a child.

keywords: Optional: Song of Storms by the corner tree
steps.detail: Go back to Hyrule Castle and sneak past the guards as before. Almost at the spot where you woke Talon with the Cucco, a small tree sits in the corner where the Shard of Agony chimes: stand next to it, play the Song of Storms to open a hole, fall down, and bomb the wall on your left for a Gold Skulltula.

keywords: Frog minigame: Song of Storms → Heart Piece #31
