steps.detail: At Zora's River's midpoint, where logs stick out of the water, stand on the square mark of the log closest to land and play your Ocarina to make five frogs appear. Play the Song of Storms and they instantly reward you with a Heart Piece. Then play each of your other non-teleport songs — Zelda's Lullaby, Saria's Song, Epona's Song, Sun's Song, Song of Time — for fifty Rupees each (worthless now, mostly wasted in your overflowing wallet); each song also grows one of the frogs.

Once all five frogs are grown, play your Ocarina again for a Simon-says game: press the button for whichever frog has a fly (butterfly) over its head; too slow or a wrong pick and you restart. The pattern — 3DS: L, X, Y, R, X, Y, R, L, R, L, R, Y, X, L; N64: A, C-left, C-right, C-down, C-left, C-right, C-down, A, C-down, A, C-down, C-right, C-left, A. Finish it for another Heart Piece.

keywords: Windmill: Song of Storms drains the well
steps.detail: Navi harasses you about the well — in the future it's blocked off, but in the past it's full of water, and the windmill draws that water. Inside the Windmill, stand before Guru-Guru and play the Song of Storms: it goes haywire (making his future reaction make sense) and the well's water drains dramatically, opening the mini-dungeon. Go back outside and climb down the well.

keywords: Crawl in, kill the Big Skulltula
steps.detail: Crawl through the hole and down the ladder. A Big Skulltula blocks the path — wait for its back to turn and kill it. Check the nearby skeleton if you like. The whole dungeon is full of fake walls, and the one straight ahead is walkable — go through it. (The primary treasure is the Lens of Truth, gettable fairly early, after which you could leave immediately; this guide grabs the Lens first, then the optional content.)

keywords: Zelda's Lullaby at the Triforce drains the water
steps.detail: Main Room — a doughnut-shaped room with a hidden room in the middle and hidden floor holes everywhere that drop you into the Basement. Make for the far side: run forward to a small stream, take the path right and follow the stream — staying on the water path you won't fall through a hole (a massive Green Bubble circles here; stun it with the Boomerang and kill it if you like). At the opposite side, play Zelda's Lullaby on the ground Triforce symbol to drain the dungeon's water, opening new areas.

keywords: Crawl through, kill the ceiling Skulltula
steps.detail: Head back to where you entered, again staying on the outer path where the water was. With the water gone, drop into the small pit at the room's entrance, open the small chest for bombs, and crawl through the small hole. Watch for the Skulltula hanging from the ceiling — kill it, climb the vines, and go through the door to the mini-boss.

Dead Hand — a creepy new enemy. Let one of its hands grab you and mash A and B to break free; wait for the body to lean down toward you and smack its head with the sword right before it bites. It walks off and burrows; repeat. Creepy but easy — beatable without a hit. The large chest that appears holds the Lens of Truth! (You could leave now, but keep following for all the goodies.)

keywords: Optional: Lens reveals a hidden chest (Gold Rupee)
steps.detail: Put the Lens of Truth to use right away: it reveals another treasure chest hidden in this room — open it for a Gold Rupee.

keywords: Drop the central X-pole hole to the Basement
steps.detail: Back in the Main Room, the Lens now shows all the fake walls. For the Dungeon Map, drop to the Basement via the hole right in the center — use the Lens to find the central area with the X-shaped poles and drop in. The Basement splits into four dead-end paths (two hold nothing; one has a green spot where the Sun's Song summons a fairy). Take the far-right path with the two burning torches: Bomb the two boulders, Sun's Song to stun the ReDead, and reach the chest with the Dungeon Map.

keywords: Basement Silver Rupees, climb back out
steps.detail: Back at the Basement's green poison-water hub, kill the lone ReDead so it doesn't bother you. Grab the three Silver Rupees near the planks, climb the ladder for another Rupee, up the second ladder for the final Silver Rupee to open the door, and go through (it closes behind you). If you ever fall back into the Basement, you redo this to get back up.

In the center room within the Main Room, a large chest sits in a fenced alcove — the Lens shows a pit blocking the entrance, so don't try to jump it. One of the side walls is fake: walk around the outside and in to grab the Compass, then leave the safe way.

Now for Small Keys, starting with the oddball: on the west side, a pit with metal grating (water-filled before) — jump down, open the small chest if you like, climb the wall and go through the door. In the Coffin Room, kill the Gibdo first (Sun's Song stuns it). Light the torches beside the coffins with Deku Sticks to open them — the southwest casket holds the Small Key; the rest have Keese and another Gibdo. Grab the key and return to the Main Room.

keywords: Two south-corner wall chests: Small Keys ×2
steps.detail: Your map shows two chests hidden in the walls of the southern corners of the Main Room. Walk the perimeter with the Lens of Truth to find the little alcoves and open both for two more Small Keys.

keywords: Optional: left center room — Gold Skulltula #79
steps.detail: The two center rooms have locked doors hiding Skulltulas. Left room: a lone Deku Baba and some killer flying pots — shield forward and deflect the projectiles before assaulting the plant. The Skulltula is along the back wall. Snag it and head to the other room.

keywords: Optional: right center room — Gold Skulltula #80
steps.detail: Right room: stand by the door and pick off the many Keese from a distance, then use the Lens of Truth to see the invisible walkway across to the Skulltula prize.

keywords: NE crawl hole to the last locked door
steps.detail: In the northeast corner, crawl through the small hole to a locked door and spend your last Small Key. The Pit Room is full of vicious pits and Fire Keese: equip the Hylian Shield so they don't burn the other one, use the Lens to see the pits and creep forward, letting the Keese hit your shield before slashing them. Lens-aim a Bomb at the Beamos to kill it (the corner chest holds a replacement Deku Shield if you lost yours). Cross to the far door.

keywords: Optional: Like Like Cage — Gold Skulltula #81
steps.detail: This tiny room is hard to move in and the Like Like will likely eat you at least once — circle it, target, and stab quickly, keeping your distance. The chest here holds a replacement shield if the Like Like or the Pit Room took yours. Snag the Skulltula on the wall. (For completionists, three more small chests remain, all in the Main Room.)

keywords: Optional: night — Treasure Chest Game with the Lens
steps.detail: One more Heart Piece before the Shadow Temple. As a child, return to the Market and play the Sun's Song for night, then play the Treasure Chest Game on the side of the market toward Hyrule Field. It normally advances you through rooms picking one of two chests at 50% odds — nearly impossible fairly. But pay the man and use the Lens of Truth in each room to see which chest holds the Small Key, so you never fail. Reach the very end for the Heart Piece.

keywords: Warp with the Nocturne of Shadow
Din's Fire to light all torches
steps.detail: You need the Nocturne of Shadow and the Lens of Truth for the Shadow Temple. Become an adult again and warp to the Graveyard with the Nocturne of Shadow, then head down. Stand on the circle platform and use Din's Fire to light all the torches — the only point in the game where it's actually required. All lit, the door opens. Yippee!

keywords: Lens the skull plate to a hidden door
steps.detail: Entry Room — a pit greets you: Longshot across and walk to the fake wall, where a voice says you need the "eye of truth" to progress (if you don't have the Lens of Truth, see the previous chapter). Through the fake wall is a puzzle for later; for now turn left and use the Lens of Truth to see through one of the skull plates in the wall to a hidden corridor and door. Go through.

keywords: Taunting Rooms: follow the right wall
Dungeon Map Room: Sun's Song the ReDead
steps.detail: The Taunting Rooms of Illusion are full of voices speaking of hatred and death — ignore them and follow the right wall to a door. In the Dungeon Map Room, quickly play the Sun's Song to stun the ReDead and kill it, plus two Keese. All dead, the chest holds the Dungeon Map. Loot the jars and exit.

Follow the right wall again into the hidden corridor between the two jars, and again in the second half of the room (floating jars on the left), to the Hover Boots Room. Another Dead Hand, with a twist: use the Lens to see where he's burrowed, move away from where he'll pop up, let the hands grab you, then confront him — easily beaten now that you do more damage, especially with the Biggoron's Sword. The chest holds the Hover Boots. Backtrack several rooms toward the entrance.

keywords: Aim the bird statue at the real skull (Lens)
steps.detail: Back in the Entry Room, approach the bird statue: it wants its beak pointed the right way. With the Lens, all the skull torches are fake except one real skull (in one of three spots, differing per game). Push or pull the statue to point at the real one and step away — right choice, the gate across opens. Equip the Hover Boots and run across, just barely grabbing the tongue. Down the hallway.

keywords: Compass Room: Sun's Song the Gibdos
steps.detail: The Beamos Corridor splits four ways. You can kill the middle Beamos (it respawns on re-entry; if killed, the Song of Storms facing the middle makes a fairy). The left and right walls are fake — take the right path to the Compass Room: two Gibdos, stunned with the Sun's Song and killed from behind. The chest holds the Compass.

Back at the Beamos, cross to the door behind the other fake wall: the Scythe Shortcut Room, with a giant grim-reaper statue of spinning scythes like the Ice Cavern's blade. Duck (R) or roll to avoid the blades and collect all five Silver Rupees — two in the middle, two in alcoves on either side, and one atop a stack of logs (Longshot on, then jump off to grab it). All five opens a nearby cell's grating: the chest holds a Small Key. Back to the previous room.

keywords: Small Key into the Guillotine Cavern
steps.detail: Two walls were fake, so the Beamos room is suspicious — Bomb the remaining wall to reveal a hidden locked door (its shape on the map gives it away). Spend your only Small Key to go through.

keywords: Guillotine Cavern: Longshot the ceiling Skulltulas
steps.detail: Go slowly down the ramped "S"-shaped hall — three Skulltulas drop from the ceiling; Longshot them fast. At the end, jump down, run through the first guillotine, kill the Skulltula, run through the second. Round the corner, Navi warns of a Wallmaster (only in this corner — kill it or run past). Then a series of guillotines on floating platforms: the Hover Boots actually make it harder (momentum slips you into blades); the third has a Red Bubble popping out — shield it (it leaves on its own), then jump to the final platform.

keywords: Lens the invisible platforms to a door
steps.detail: A Stalfos appears — use the small space to stab him repeatedly so he backs into the bottomless pit (Song of Storms makes a fairy here). Before jumping down, turn left and use the Lens to see invisible platforms; jump/hover along them following the outer wall to a door.

keywords: Optional: Invisible Scythe Room — Gold Skulltula #82
steps.detail: A small room with an invisible spinning grim-reaper statue: use the Lens to avoid the blades, circle the room killing the wall Keese and the Like Like across the way. All dead, the grating opens to two chests (one invisible — Lens to see it) with Arrows and a replacement Shield (or just blue rupees). Kill the Skulltula, snag the token, and return.

Jump back across the invisible platforms to where the Stalfos reappears; kill him, then find the rising-and-falling platform on the other side. Wait for the nearby platform to fall really low, jump on (Hover Boots risky — slipperiness), ride it to its highest point and jump down to the lower area on the right. Follow the skinny bridge to a Beamos and two circling Blade Traps: five Silver Rupees here — one near the ledge, one against the wall, two between the Blade Traps, one under the Beamos. All five lifts the grating.

keywords: Spikey Falling Trap Room: push the "stone umbrella" block
steps.detail: Stepping under a smashing spiked platform restarts the room, and you can't sprint through. The sign says you need a "stone umbrella" — glad I packed one. Use the Lens to find a stone block in a hidden cubby, pull it out and push it under the traps, positioned right between two spikes (there's a floor mark for the exact spot) so you can navigate safely. One side alcove's chest holds arrows; behind the fence on the left side is a Gold Skulltula.

keywords: Block to the edge, hop the spiked platforms
steps.detail: Run to the end and pull the block to the edge, then jump on top of it and onto the falling spiked platforms. A chest behind the gate holds rupees; a floor switch on this side makes another chest appear — cross via the other spiked platform and open it for a Small Key. Return to the big cavern.

keywords: Middle land: Lens the invisible platforms
steps.detail: In the cavern's middle is a stretch of land with a single guillotine leading to a seeming dead end — but a door lies across the gap. Use the Lens to see two invisible platforms (one moving) and reach the locked door. Inside the Invisible Spike Room: three sets of invisible spikes and two ReDeads — Lens to avoid the spikes, Sun's Song to stun the ReDeads (their chest holds a measly five Rupees). Then gather all Silver Rupees: first in the middle, second and third against the walls (Longshot the targets above), and the last two together in mid-air in the corner — Lens shows one is on a little platform; Longshot the invisible target, then Hover Boots to the final Rupee. Go through the door that opens.

keywords: First Giant Skull Room: kill the Keese
Bomb Flower the skull: Small Key + Gold Skulltula #84
steps.detail: Kill the four Keese (two above the entrance touch blue torches to become Fire Keese — Fairy Bow them quickly). Climb either set of ledges and finish the rest. Toss a Bomb Flower into the giant flaming skull until one lands: the skull explodes and a Small Key pops out. Drop down, collect it, turn around, and snag the Gold Skulltula that was hiding behind the skull. Return to the previous room.

keywords: Lens the ceiling target, Longshot up
steps.detail: Back in the Invisible Spike Room, Lens to see a ceiling target and Longshot up to the locked door. The Fan Corridor beyond has Spike Traps and fans turning on and off — run when the fans are off, or just wear the Iron Boots. Round the first corner and inch forward to make a Skulltula appear; Longshot it. At the next corner a pit: wait for the fan and Hover across, or (easier) Iron Boots on and Longshot the beam across.

keywords: Wind Room: Iron Boots past the fans
Hover-run the fan to the far side
steps.detail: Drop into a large room with a central bridge and wall-mounted fans trying to blow you off — Iron Boots walk past them. A trick Eye Switch above the far door shoots deflectable flames; ignore it or Bow it when it opens (an optional side door has two ReDeads, a hint picture, a Lens-hidden arrow chest, and a Sun's-Song fairy spot). Use the Lens to find the fake wall on one side in the lane nearest the door: wait for the fan to stop, stand before it, equip the Hover Boots, resist until the wind is full force, then run WITH it to cross safely. Enter the door.

keywords: Gibdo Room: Sun's Song, Bombs chest
Lens the invisible chest: Small Key
steps.detail: In this dark room, play the Sun's Song to stun the Gibdos and clear them; a small chest holds Bombs — foreshadowing! The map shows another chest in the corner: blow up the funny dirt pile with the red crack to reveal… nothing. Suspicious — use the Lens to find the invisible chest holding a Small Key. Enter the locked door.

keywords: Boat Room: slot the stone block
steps.detail: In this large room, turn left and pull back the giant stone block, then push/pull it along the path and click it into the slot next to the ladder. This lets you proceed and opens a shortcut back to here — if you ever die, you return via a secret passage in the first grim-reaper-scythe room. Nifty.

keywords: Optional: Scarecrow's Song + Longshot — Gold Skulltula #85
steps.detail: Climb the ladder onto the boat, then look left above the shortcut area you opened: a Gold Skulltula sits on that ledge, and Navi flies up green. Play the Scarecrow's Song and Longshot from the boat to reach it. Collect the token and jump down.

Stand on the Triforce symbol on the boat and play Zelda's Lullaby: the boat moves and two Stalfos come in a row (target one and the other mostly leaves you alone — get in their face and stab repeatedly, as they drop their guard, or just play defense; you don't strictly need to kill them). When the boat hits the wall at the end and starts to fall into the abyss, jump off onto the platform on the left first. Enter the door on your right.

keywords: Invisible Maze: clockwise from the south door
steps.detail: The Invisible Maze has invisible walls and a door in each corner, plus two annoying Floormasters in the middle (they respawn on re-entry). Use the Lens to see the walls and work clockwise from the south door. In the Invisible Floormaster Room, whip out the Lens for the invisible Floormaster (you can't Spin Attack and Lens at once) — kill the big hand, then target and stab the smaller ones. The chest holds a Small Key. Return to the maze.

keywords: Optional: west door — Second Skull Room Gold Skulltula #86
steps.detail: Go clockwise to the west door: the Second Skull Room has three spinning skulls (blow them up for random magic jars, hearts, arrows and rupees). The only real prize is the dungeon's final Gold Skulltula, hidden behind the skulls.

keywords: Din's Fire the spike traps, chest: Boss Key
steps.detail: Back to the maze and the last door, north: the Boss Key Room has two wooden spike traps that slowly crush you — burn them with Din's Fire (Fire Arrows don't work here). Kill the ReDead on either side and open the colorful chest for the Boss Key. Return to the large open room where you jumped off the boat.

keywords: Arrow the bomb flower to topple the pillar
steps.detail: You should have one Small Key. Across the abyss are bomb flowers next to a large pillar: fire an arrow at the bomb flower to blow the statue's base and topple it across as a bridge. "Tiiiiimmmbeeeeeerrr!" Cross over — the Song of Time at the broken pillar's base gets recovery hearts on the right, and the Scarecrow's Song reaches a high platform with more hearts. Enter the locked door.

keywords: Invisible Platform Room: Lens + Hover Boots across
steps.detail: The final room has invisible odd-shaped pillars far apart: use the Lens to see them and the Hover Boots to cross (left or right side, a running start makes it easily). Enter the boss door and fall down the pit.

keywords: Stun both hands, Lens + shoot the eye, slash
steps.detail: One of the hardest bosses — all timing and quick reaction, big damage; bring a couple of fairies. The fight is on a giant bongo drum floating in poison (jump back on fast if cast off; the Hover Boots make it harder). Two hands punch predictably; the fast swipe is hard to dodge — hold R to duck when not targeting, roll to avoid the rest. Stun BOTH hands to make him vulnerable — the Bow is by far the best. Method 1: stand back to see both, arrow one, dodge the other and stab it if it lingers after a ground slam, otherwise arrow it as it returns to start. Method 2 (great if out of arrows): wait for one hand to attack, run for the still hand and smack it, then turn to where the other returns and smack it as it arrives. With both hands stunned, use the Lens to see Bongo Bongo: he opens his eye and rushes you with hands on either side — hit the open eye to stun him (Bow easiest, Sword or Longshot work), then go to town with the sword. With the Biggoron's Sword he can be over within a minute; defeated, he boils down into shadow goo.

missable.deadline: During this fight — Invisible One requires beating Bongo Bongo without the Lens of Truth or being trapped by his hands; Invisible Stalker adds no healing, with 3 hearts only

keywords: Receive the Shadow Medallion from Impa
steps.detail: Snag the Heart Container, then head for the blue portal. In the Chamber of Sages, Impa reminisces about Ganondorf's attack on Hyrule Castle, explains he was really after the Ocarina of Time (which we knew), and assures you Zelda is safe and that you'll meet her when the time is right. She gives you the Shadow Medallion — just one more!
Next destination: the desert, due west. It's a fair way across Hyrule Field — use Epona to speed the trip.

The Gerudo have broken the bridge to keep people out. Cross by running at it with Epona, or Longshot the hanging bridge pieces or the target directly above each side. Talk to the man here: the Gerudo women broke the bridge and his construction workers ran off to their fortress; he'll fix the bridge if you get his workers back. We don't need the bridge, but this quest is required to reach the next major destination.

Two Skulltulas here — play the Sun's Song for night. #87 is directly behind the Carpenters' tent, on the back wall (Longshot it). #88 is up near the top of the archway just to the left after you cross the bridge, on the south side — follow your ears. Then continue to the fortress ahead.

A short scene surveys the area, then you're free to roam — but the moment a female guard sees you, you're captured and thrown in an inescapable cell. They didn't take your items, though: Longshot the wooden overhang in the window to escape. (Captured again later, you repeat this.)

Escaped, stun the nearest guard with the Longshot or Bow, or wait until she's away, drop down and enter the door on your left. Round the corner to a carpenter in a cell; he and his fellows were captured and will repay you. A Gerudo Thief interrupts — a new enemy that fights instead of jailing you: block with your shield, back off if she "charges up" (her jump attack pierces your defense and knocks you out), and target-plus-jump-attack her (it lands almost always). Beaten, she drops a Small Key — free Ichiro. Three carpenters to go.

Through the next opening you're back outside; enter the door right nearby. This room is a hub to many other doors, most guarded — turn right as soon as you can and out the next opening (safer than fighting through). Outside, target around the corner, shoot the guard with an arrow when she's turned away, jump the small ledge and enter the doorway. Battle the Gerudo Thief, open the cell, and free the second carpenter, Jiro. Two left!

Back outside by another opening, notice the vines leading down nearby: climb down and enter the only door on this level. Talk to the worker, slay the Gerudo Thief, and release him. Just one left — go back the way you came.

Climb up the vines and through the door on the left, into a room with guards below who can't see you on the ledge — Bow the two guards, or Hover Boots across the platform to the far side, and follow the path out. Outside, ignore the little ledge next to you and turn the opposite way, dropping into the little area with the isolated door. Inside, shoot the guard from behind the crate, round the corner to the last carpenter, and fight the final Gerudo Thief. Shiro shares a secret he overheard before running off.

All four carpenters rescued, the fortress leader appears: all men are worthless… except "The Great Ganondorf" and now yourself, for besting her guards. Nabooru, second only to Ganondorf, put her in charge, and your talent earns you a place in their all-female thieves' guild — the Gerudo Membership Card (Gerudo Token on 3DS).

keywords: Optional: night — Gold Skulltula #89 on the back wall
steps.detail: (This whole goodies section is optional.) Play the Sun's Song for night and work to the top of the fortress via the guard room. Out that exit, climb onto the little ledge, follow it and jump onto the roof to your right, around the bend, up the vines, along the highest roof to the end — a Gold Skulltula sits directly below on the back wall. Longshot it.
A chest sits in the distance: go right up to the edge and Longshot it, or play the Scarecrow's Song and Hookshot over. Open the large chest for a Heart Piece.

Make it daytime, summon Epona, and ride to the back shooting range; talk to the woman in white while mounted to play. Epona moves on her own, freeing you to aim: take the jars first (hold still, 100 points each), turn left and shoot the big target once or twice, then on the way back hit any missed jars and the three wall targets — perfect shots 100, close 60; align vertically with a target and ease the stick left to repeat-hit it, and spend every arrow at the end (seven mediocre beats one or two perfect). Score 1000+ for a Piece of a Heart (always the first reward), and 1500+ for the Biggest Quiver.

keywords: Optional: night — Gold Skulltula #90 on the far target
steps.detail: With the heart piece and Big Quiver, play the Sun's Song for night and return to the shooting range: a Gold Skulltula waits on the lone far target at the end. Longshot it.

Ready for the desert. (The Gerudo Training Grounds mini-dungeon is optional and covered next chapter, when we have every item.) Head to the northwest of the fortress to a giant gate, climb the ladder and speak with the woman in white — she warns of two trials ahead, claps the gate open, and bids you goodbye. Hop down and through.

Play the Sun's Song for daytime — it's easier to see. Run forward, jump atop the box, and cross the giant quicksand pit by Longshotting the boxes on the far side. Then follow the flags as the Gerudo said; a sign points to a dock where a man on a magic carpet sells Bombchus (you don't need them; Hover Boots reach him if you want some). Keep following the flags one to the next — stray and the area restarts.

keywords: Optional: Gold Skulltula #91 in the stone building
steps.detail: At the end stands a stone building — jump down inside, away from the sand. Kill the Skulltula waiting between the two torches and snag its token. (Lighting the torches with Din's Fire or Fire Arrows gives a measly 50 Rupees.) Smash the jars and go back up.

Climb onto the stone building by the side ramp and check the stone. Use the Lens of Truth at the top to reveal a Big Poe that guides you the rest of the way: follow its winding path through the poles at the desert's end, keeping the Lens on the whole time (a full magic bar makes it comfortable).

Ahh, able to see again. Cross to the temple straight ahead, avoiding the annoying Leevers (you can't miss it). Inside, shield against the killer jars, then find you can do nothing yet: the entry room has two Armos, a hole you can't crawl through on the left, a giant block you can't push on the right, a ceiling hole you can't use, and two snake-like statues below. It needs a child to crawl the hole — go back outside.

keywords: Learn the Requiem of Spirit from Sheik
steps.detail: Sheik leaps down with more poetry about using the Ocarina of Time to return here as a child, then teaches your next song — a warp directly to the Desert Colossus, since you can't cross the Gerudo area as a child. (Sheik keeps Link at a distance… what is she hiding?) With this, you now know all twelve songs.

(Everything else in this section is optional — to press on, become a child and enter the temple.) Get a Magic Bean from the Salesman near Zora's River first, then play the Prelude of Light to the Temple of Time, replace the sword to become a child, and warp back to the Desert Colossus. The Leevers are as big as you now. Drop bugs on the soft soil near the Spirit Temple entrance for a Gold Skulltula (bugs are under the small rock dead center of the desert, by the two boulders), and plant a Magic Bean here for later.

Return as an adult and ride the grown Magic Bean plant: it takes you onto the large rock at the north-central desert, where a Gold Skulltula sits at night (Sun's Song). Riding the bean plant also circles the entire desert and passes beneath the stone arc near the Spirit Temple entrance — as it heads back up, jump off onto the arc to grab a Heart Piece.

keywords: Optional: night — Gold Skulltula #94 at the Oasis palms
steps.detail: Near the Oasis at the south end of the map is a trio of palm trees: at night, Longshot the Gold Skulltula near the top of one.

Near the Colossus entrance, two lone trees stand against a cracked wall — Bomb it open. Inside is another Fairy Fountain: play Zelda's Lullaby on the Triforce to summon the third and final Great Fairy of Magic, who grants Nayru's Love — a shield against attacks for a little under a minute (it consumes magic, so use it wisely).

keywords: Optional: Song of Storms fills the Fairy Oasis
steps.detail: At the south, a dried-up oasis: jump on the rock so the Leevers leave you alone and play the Song of Storms — it slowly fills the oasis, which then continually spawns red fairies. Stock up, then enter the Spirit Temple as a child.

keywords: Agree to get the Silver Gauntlets
steps.detail: Entry Room — shield the floating jars, up the stairs, ignore the Armos, head left to the hole where a Gerudo woman blocks you. Before naming herself she asks a few questions: answer that you're "not doing anything" to progress (the second answer doesn't matter). She tasks you with retrieving the Silver Gauntlets — agree — and moves aside. Crawl through, leaving Nabooru behind.

keywords: Main Corridor: kill the flyers, then the Armos
steps.detail: This temple is full of fire enemies (Fire Keese and the new Anubis) and it's the last portion you must do as a child; keep the Hylian Shield equipped to protect the Deku Shield. This room has two Spike Traps, two Fire Keese, two Keese and an Armos: kill the flyers first, then bomb the Armos (the Boomerang stuns it too). Both doors open — despite it pointing right, enter the LEFT door.

keywords: Stalfos Room: beat the Stalfos + Green Bubble
Boomerang the switch, drop the bridge
steps.detail: A Stalfos — harder as a child (tougher to avoid, less damage dealt); slash and dodge its jump attack, and try to make it walk off the edge into the abyss. Then a giant Green Bubble: Boomerang to stun, Slingshot to finish. To drop the bridge across the gap, Boomerang the crystal switch on the far side — aim left so it wraps around and hits the target. Across, open the chest for a new Deku Shield, then through the door.

keywords: First Anubis Room: burn it into the flame
steps.detail: The Anubis copies your every move and shoots fire if you use a weapon; it's only vulnerable to fire. The crystal switch on the right lights a flame by the other door: maneuver so the Anubis floats right next to that door, then Slingshot the switch (or get close and use Din's Fire). Bam! Cross back and through the door.

Navi warns of a Wallmaster — slay the lone Fire Keese, then the Wallmaster. Collect the five Silver Rupees, taking the fence ones first but NOT touching the Gold Skulltula (it hurts you through the fence); the final Rupee by the corner torch drops the gate. Kill the incoming Keese one at a time, and Boomerang the Gold Skulltula token now. Then light the two unlit torches (Deku Stick from the lit torch, or Din's Fire) to make a chest with a Small Key appear back in the other area. Grab it and go through.
Back in the room with the lone Spike Trap, crawl through the small hole in the north wall to a small area with a locked door, and go through with your Small Key.

Turn right and kill the two Skullwalltulas on the climbable wall, climb up, then turn around and Boomerang the Gold Skulltula waiting directly behind where you climbed. Two Lizalfos appear as you walk certain spots — wander until they show and kill both. For the puzzle you need Bombchus (low? shoot the crystal switch atop the south tiered ledge for two chests, one with Bombchus). A sun face in the middle of the floor needs sunlight: the cracked wall has light peeking through — line up, target it and send a Bombchu. Boom! Through the door.

keywords: Colossus Room (child): Armos on the switch
steps.detail: A giant snake-woman statue dominates one wall (like the temple's exterior); nothing to do with it yet. By the entrance, an Armos and a switch below that opens the door above but must be held: push the Armos onto it. Climb the stairs and through the door, round the red stairs and through the next door too.

keywords: 5 Silver Rupees, light torches: Small Key
Push the Sun Face block into the light
steps.detail: Kill the three Beamos with Bombs, minding the Spike Traps. Collect five Silver Rupees from the Beamos niches — one on the south wall, two in the center (one requires a jump), two on the north wall. All five lights the golden torch; light all the torches (clockwise is easiest) and climb to the chest for a Small Key. Finally, pull the center blocks aside and bring the Sun Face block under the sunlight to open the far door.

keywords: Corridor 2: Gold Skulltula #97 above the door
steps.detail: Just a set of stairs — but turn around and look above the door you came through for the Gold Skulltula. Snag it, then through the next door.

Smack the throned statue with any weapon to wake it: an Iron Knuckle, hard the first time given the sword's short range. Lure it into a pillar or the throne to smash out three recovery hearts (roll past to grab them). The trick: bait an attack, jump back, and stab it quickly while stunned, then back away; repeat until it falls. Nayru's Love protects you if needed, and Bombchus hit it from range. Beaten, through the open door into the sunlight.

keywords: Desert Colossus Right Hand: chest — Silver Gauntlets
steps.detail: Outside on the Colossus's right hand, Kaepora Gaebora offers advice about the coming boss, then leaves. Open the chest for the Silver Gauntlets! A short scene shows Nabooru abducted by the Twinrova sisters. Nothing left here as a child.

keywords: Push the silver block, crystal switch opens both doors
steps.detail: With the Silver Gauntlets you can progress deeper — as an adult. Play the Prelude of Light to the Temple of Time, become an adult again, and re-enter the dungeon. Rush past the killer jars, up the steps, and push the giant silver block on the right until it drops into the hole. The revealed section has a Beamos and Spike Trap (both ignorable). Longshot or Bow the crystal switch hanging from the center ceiling to unlock both doors; enter the left one first.

Kill the Wolfos, then stand on the floor Triforce and play Zelda's Lullaby to make a large chest appear — Longshot to it for the Compass. Drop into the sandy area, climb up and out, then enter the opposite (right) door.

keywords: Optional: Song of Time Gold Skulltula #98
steps.detail: Three boulders roll in this bowled room; grab the five Silver Rupees — four in the cubbies (two per side), the fifth in the middle (climb up and Hover Boots to it). That opens the far door. Quickly, play the Song of Time at the Time Block to move it over an alcove and snag the Gold Skulltula behind it.

keywords: Small Key Room: kill the Like Like
steps.detail: A small chest room — a Like Like drops from the ceiling; kill it, then open the chest for a Small Key. Backtrack two rooms to the Beamos/Spike-Trap room and use the key on its locked door.

keywords: Trap Room: Like Like + jars, invisible Floormaster (Lens)
steps.detail: Traps abound: a ceiling Like Like and two attacking jars on the right — beat them, then climb the grooved wall. An invisible Floormaster lurks (you can hear it): Lens it out, kill the big hand, then flip off the Lens and Spin Attack the little ones. Grab the mirror and move it to light the Sun Faces — the first two drop chests (the SECOND is a trap chest that freezes you), the third unlocks the door; pushing to the fourth spawns a Wallmaster. Through the opened door.

keywords: Colossus Room (adult): Zelda's Lullaby on the left hand
Longshot the other hand: Small Key
steps.detail: Back in the giant room — avoid this Armos (it comes alive); a target directly above lets you Longshot back up if you fall. Up the stairs, jump with the Hover Boots to the Colossus's near (left) hand bearing a Triforce symbol and play Zelda's Lullaby to make two chests appear. One is on the other hand — Longshot across and open the small chest for a Small Key.

keywords: Din's Fire the torches: Dungeon Map
steps.detail: From the top of the west side, look to the northwest corner for an unreachable Gold Skulltula — Navi turns green, so play the Scarecrow's Song for Pierre, Longshot over and claim the token (just one more after this!). Then drop to the middle, light both torches with Din's Fire or Fire Arrows, and open the large chest for the Dungeon Map.

keywords: Hammer the rusty switch, drop through
steps.detail: In the southeast corner, look across the gap for a chest that appeared with your Zelda's Lullaby. Longshot to it, then Megaton-Hammer the rusty switch to open the barred door in the middle of the room. Drop down and head through.

keywords: Shortcut Room: silver blocks into holes, hammer the switch
steps.detail: Push the giant silver blocks into the holes, then smash the rusty switch at the end to make an elevator that runs to the dungeon's entrance room — a handy shortcut. Head back to the Colossus Room.
Work up to the southeast corner, use the Small Key on the locked door, and climb the Beamos-guarded stairs beyond (bomb it or run past).

keywords: Second Anubis Room: fire circle clockwise
steps.detail: Easiest with Din's Fire or Fire Arrows (instant kills); low on magic, do it manually. Bomb the nearby Beamos first, then play the Song of Time at the green-marked pit on the right to make a Time Block. Three Anubis mirror you into the central fire: start the fire and follow the wall clockwise (Link on the Time Block, by the locked door, by the entry door) to lead all three in at once. Through the opened door.

keywords: Armos Switch Room: lure an Armos onto the switch
steps.detail: The central switch opens the barred door on the right but must be held: stand near that door and Bow or Longshot one of the four surrounding Armos on the far side to make it jump onto the switch, then rush through while it's open.

keywords: Second Iron Knuckle Room: chests via Lens
steps.detail: In the hallway, Lens the Truth for two hidden chests, then up the stairs to another armored soldier — as an adult you do more damage and have more range, so a few strikes (or the Biggoron's Sword) fell him quickly. The door behind the throne opens; step into the light.

keywords: Desert Colossus Left Hand: chest — Mirror Shield
steps.detail: Outside on the Colossus's left hand, a large chest thumps down: open it for the Mirror Shield. (Remember to switch back to the Hylian Shield to reflect ordinary physical attacks like Octorok shots.) Return to the four-Armos room.

keywords: Mirror Shield onto the Sun Face
steps.detail: This time cross to the opposite door: equip the Mirror Shield and reflect the sunlight onto the Sun Face to open it. Inside, snag the Small Key — you brace for a trap, but there isn't one. Return through the previous room to the Anubis room and enter its locked door.

keywords: Moving Trap Wall: Longshot to the top
steps.detail: Kill the two Beamos first, then the Skullwalltula. You can skip the whole puzzle by Longshotting straight to the top wall; or climb carefully — get your lower hand just over an edge while the upper grips the moving wall above, wait until the wall heads your way, then hold up on the stick and run for the next chunk. At the top, Longshot a platform for recovery hearts if needed, clear the two Beamos, and through the door.

keywords: Triforce Corridor: Zelda's Lullaby opens the door
steps.detail: Play Zelda's Lullaby on the floor Triforce symbol to open the barred door straight ahead.

keywords: Boss Key Room: Fire Slugs, bomb the fake door
steps.detail: Kill all the Fire Slugs, then Bomb the fake door directly left of the flaming chest to reveal a gold eye switch. Arrow it to make an icy platform appear above; Longshot atop the first platform, jump to the second, and stand on the switch to stop the flames briefly. Drop down and open the fancy chest for the Boss Key!

Return and enter the plain door on the right. Killer jars fly at you — avoid the ceramics. Hit the crystal switch (on the ground in 3DS; beyond the bars but still hittable on N64) and go through. A Lizalfos greets you — dice it, then use the sunlight to light the Sun Face above the arch and open a small chest of Bombs.

Kill all the White Bubbles (the Bow suits their jerky movement). Your map shows a room to the west — Bomb the odd wall to reveal it. Now the mirrors: aim the first through the secret opening into the darkly lit room, then turn the second so it shines through the bars onto the final round mirror. Go back through the door.

keywords: Mirror Shield onto the Sun Face
steps.detail: Drop to the lower area and defend against the killer jars. The light now reflects off the mirror above — use the Mirror Shield to shine it onto the nearby Sun Face, lowering your platform.

keywords: Colossus Room: reflect light onto the statue's head
steps.detail: Back at the giant statue — it's obvious enough: reflect the light onto the statue's head to crumble it to bits. Longshot the bars across the way to move them aside, then enter the Boss Door when ready.

keywords: Almost Boss Room: beat the Iron Knuckle
steps.detail: Koume and Kotake huddle around a familiar foe, then send an Iron Knuckle after you — this one looks slightly different, and even Navi senses something off. Smash it to pieces and the armor falls off to reveal a girl: Nabooru, plopped out and dazed. The Twinrova sisters snatch her away again and vanish. Time to rescue her once more — enter the final door.

keywords: Reflect beams sister-to-sister, then absorb 3 same-element
steps.detail: Stand in the middle of the big platform to start. Phase 1: the sisters circle and take turns firing slow beams of opposite elements — keep moving to dodge (each does ~2 hearts and leaves a lingering damage zone; ice freezes briefly, fire hurts a lot — roll to douse). Use the Mirror Shield to reflect one sister's beam at the OTHER: wait on the far side, face the one about to shoot, and as the beam nears, point the shield or switch targets to the opposing sister — close enough, it damages her. After several hits they merge into Twinrova. Phase 2: now each beam CHARGES your shield, and absorbing the opposite element bursts energy back on you — so collect only the SAME element: block Fire with the shield, but run from Ice (and vice versa). Once you've absorbed three of one element, the shield releases the energy — run at her and point it (turn left or R); close and in time, she drops onto one of the four platforms. Jump over and slash her (a mid-air B jump attack on the way over works but is tricky). Repeat to finish her.

missable.deadline: During this fight — Twins of Evil requires beating the Twinrova Sisters without falling from the platform or reviving; Double Trouble requires only the Master Sword and Mirror Shield, no Fairies, 3 hearts only

keywords: Receive the Spirit Medallion from Nabooru
steps.detail: The sisters bicker pointlessly and vanish, promising "I'll come back to haunt you!" — foreshadowing another Zelda title. Grab the Heart Container on one side of the room, then step onto the portal. Nabooru turns out to be the Sage of Spirit: impressed by your skill, she gives you the final medallion.

keywords: Warp to the Temple of Time
steps.detail: With all six sages saved, a vision of Rauru sends you to the Temple of Time — Prelude of Light there. Sheik makes a final appearance, reiterating that all six sages are gathered and only Ganondorf remains, then tells the Triforce's fate: when Ganondorf claimed it, it split — Power stayed with him, Courage went to you, and Wisdom to Sheik… who is Princess Zelda all along! She explains giving you the Ocarina of Time during her escape inadvertently served Ganondorf, and now the sages can seal him for good. She gives you the mystical Light Arrow — then an earthquake encases her in a crystal, and Ganondorf, gloating that he's caught the princess, taunts you to his castle. A few final things first.

keywords: Optional: hear all 32 Gossip Stones in one session (Mask of Truth)
steps.detail: One of the final things before Ganon, for Lifestyle and Stones: with the Mask of Truth equipped and all warp songs learned, tour every Gossip Stone in Hyrule and listen to all 32 rumors in a single session (don't reset). They're spread across the world — 7 around Kokiri Forest, the Lost Woods and Sacred Forest Meadow; 7 around Hyrule Castle (four outside the Temple of Time, the courtyard vines, the castle path, and one in the Song-of-Storms grotto outside the castle); 5 around Goron City and Death Mountain (including one inside Dodongo's Cavern); 5 along Zora's River, Domain and Fountain; 3 at Lake Hylia; 2 at Gerudo Valley; and the rest at the Graveyard (via Nocturne of Shadow) and the Desert Colossus. Use the warp songs to hop regions efficiently. The full stone-by-stone list is in the RA community guide.

keywords: Optional: night — lift the silver boulder
steps.detail: Head to Zora's Fountain, to the southeast corner where the Great Fairy is: a lone silver boulder now lifts with the Silver Gauntlets. Lift it, bomb the regular boulder beneath, and drop in. Use the Lens of Truth to navigate the passage past invisible Skulltulas on the ceiling, climb the grating at the end, and the last Gold Skulltula (night only) waits on the left wall. Snag token #100 and head for Kakariko.

keywords: 100 tokens: final Skulltula House reward (200 Rupees)
steps.detail: Check your status screen — with all 100 Skulltula tokens, enter the Skulltula House and speak with the man in the middle, who thanks you and hopes not to get cursed again. The mystical prize for all 100? A measly 200 Rupees — completely worthless, one of the dumbest prizes in gaming — but you can re-enter for it repeatedly for infinite cash.

keywords: Pay 10 Rupees to enter the Gerudo Training Ground
steps.detail: (Optional side quest.) Ride Epona from the Temple of Time to the Gerudo Fortress. With the Gerudo Membership Card you can now speak with the Gerudo women; one in white stands before a barred entrance and, for 10 Rupees, admits you to the Gerudo Training Ground — a series of puzzle rooms for keys. You need every item from every dungeon to get all nine keys, though seven suffice to reach the prize if you know what you're doing; best tackled with all items in hand.

keywords: Entrance: arrow the eye switch for two chests
steps.detail: Turn around and shoot the golden eye switch above the entrance for two small chests (a Blue Rupee and a bundle of arrows). Three doors here: the one across leads to the locked doors and main prize (last). The dungeon loops, so start left or right — left is easier.
One minute to kill both Stalfos (easy, especially with the Biggoron's Sword; keep moving on the sand). The chest holds the first Small Key. Through the door.

A Wallmaster in the middle, rolling boulders on both sides, invisible flame walls — 90 seconds for five Silver Rupees (ignore the Wallmaster). Forward and left: peek the corner, wait for the boulder, dart left for Rupee 1; right, duck the side crevices past the boulder, up top for Rupee 2. The flameless higher middle corridor, turn right, jump off the ledge for Rupee 3. Down the middle corridor's right side, wait for the boulder, clockwise around the corner for Rupee 4. Longshot the ceiling target straight ahead to drop into Rupee 5. The timer stops and the door opens — Longshot the ceiling target past the flames and through.

keywords: Lens the switch above the fake door
steps.detail: Kill the two Wolfos and two White Wolfos, then Lens a hidden area above the fake door — Longshot up and step on the switch to open both sealed doors below. Jump down and push the giant Silver Block into the hole with the Gauntlets, then enter the unsealed door.

keywords: Kill 3 Like Likes, Lens the sandy hole
steps.detail: Kill the three Like Likes to spawn two chests (the platform's original is a freeze trap; the two up front hold a Blue and Gold Rupee). Lens the last sandy hole for an invisible chest with the second Small Key. Return, Longshot back to the hidden area, and enter the door.

keywords: Shoot the statue's eyes: Small Key [3]
steps.detail: A barred door up top can't be used yet — but stand by it so Navi turns green and play the Scarecrow's Song for Pierre (easy access back up), then jump down. On the spinning outer platform, face the center statue and shoot each eye once with an arrow to make a small chest appear beside them; Longshot to it for the third Small Key. Longshot the target near this area's entrance to exit.

keywords: Scarecrow's Song to the top door
steps.detail: The top barred door is now open — reach it by falling in the lava, or facing it so Navi turns green and playing the Scarecrow's Song for Pierre. Longshot to him and through the door: a small chest with the fourth (hidden) Small Key. Back, jump down, and enter the other door.

keywords: Hammer the statues, switch: Small Key [5]
steps.detail: Kill the two Fire Keese and Fire Slugs to open the door and spawn a chest of worthless stuff. Megaton-Hammer the statues for goodies; the northeast statue hides a switch in a hole. Step on it and grab the fifth Small Key from the center chest before the fire returns. Through the next door. (Going back the other way needs the gold eye switch that opens the statue room.)

keywords: Song of Time blocks: Small Key [6]
steps.detail: Five Silver Rupees open the east door. Forward a bit, turn, Longshot the ceiling target for Rupee 1. Drop to the platform ahead for Rupee 2. Equip the Hover Boots to the middle platform; where Navi turns green, play the Song of Time for two Time Blocks — float over and climb to the sixth Small Key (don't take that entrance). Drop to the side switch to douse the fire across the room. Hover the platform path for Rupee 3, cross for Rupee 4, then back to the middle for Rupee 5 last (grabbing it last elsewhere can trigger the door animation while the lava returns, knocking you in). Enter the east door.

Play the Song of Time to remove the Time Blocks, then Iron Boots and Zora Tunic on, sink down avoiding the central Blade Trap, and Longshot the four Shell Blades. Rupee 1 is in the middle around the Blade Trap; for 2 and 3, line up with the west-wall target with a Rupee between you and pass through it onto the next; do the same on the east wall for Rupee 4; then take off the boots and float to the roof for Rupee 5.

keywords: Bomb the Beamos, kill 2 Dinalfos: Small Key [8]
steps.detail: Float across to the left door and head south. Bomb the Beamos immediately, then handle the two Dinalfos — like Lizalfos but they don't take turns when you target one, so run-and-slash them. Dead, the barred door opens and a chest gives the eighth Small Key.

keywords: Center room: Lens the ceiling for the 9th key
steps.detail: Back at the entrance (Song of Storms in the center makes a fairy), enter the locked-doors room. Eight keys in hand; from the entrance room you only need seven, but for the ninth: enter the left door and Lens the ceiling directly left — a hole with grating climbs to a secret room holding the final key. Climb back down and go through all the remaining left-side doors to reach the center and claim the prize: the Ice Arrows. Exit the mini-dungeon.

keywords: Sages form the bridge of light
steps.detail: Ready for the showdown — fill bottles with two fairies and two Blue Potions from the Magic Hag's Shop, then warp to the Temple of Time and approach Ganon's Castle. Rauru speaks telepathically: six evil barriers inside must be broken before the sages can help; they form a bridge of light to the castle. Enter the lair. Run down the steps and straight to the door, ignoring the Beamos.

The central tower is ringed by a shield fed by six colored energy beams (the six elements); break all six barriers before ascending. A hidden shop sits directly below the tower bridge in the wall — Lens the illusion to find Business Scrubs and fairies. Order mostly doesn't matter (two barriers need an item from Shadow), but do Shadow (purple) first — head through.

keywords: Shadow Cavern: Fire Arrow torches, block paths
steps.detail: Fire-Arrow the unlit torch on the right platform to make blocks appear temporarily; run them (avoid the Green Bubble, beware the Like Like spitting you into the pit) to the far platform. Re-light torches to reform blocks, jump platform to platform to the switch, which spawns a large chest on the big platform — Longshot up and open it for the Golden Gauntlets (why Shadow went first). Then Lens the invisible path to a rusty switch, Hammer it to open the door, and cross (Longshot the unlit torch or take the invisible middle path) through.

keywords: Shadow Barrier: Light Arrow the sphere (frees Impa)
steps.detail: In the final room, shoot a Light Arrow at the golden sphere to destroy the barrier binding the Sage of Shadow, Impa. She teleports you back to the main room.

keywords: Optional: Golden Gauntlets toss the outdoor pillar
Great Fairy of Courage: double defense
steps.detail: Exit the castle entirely; in the outdoor area, go all the way left to a huge silver pillar against the wall and press A — Link heaves it into the hillside. Enter the last Great Fairy Fountain and play Zelda's Lullaby to summon the Great Fairy of Courage, who grants the best upgrade in the game: double defense, halving all damage taken. Return to the castle.

keywords: Forest Barrier: Torch Room (Wolfos, light 5 torches)
steps.detail: Next the Forest Barrier (green). Torch Room: kill the Wolfos, then light all five torches (four center, one above the door) with Din's Fire and/or Fire Arrows. Silver Rupee & Fan Room — mind the pits (a fall restarts it): Hover Boots on, play the Song of Time facing the Rupee on your left, ride the fan into Rupee 1 onto the mid-left platform, Bomb the central Beamos, land on the switch platform and Longshot back to the target for the confusing Rupee 2, use the center pillar across for Rupee 3, head to the barred door for Rupee 4, and climb up and wait out the right fan for Rupee 5. Through the door.
Refill magic from the jars, then Light-Arrow the barrier to free the Sage of Forest, Saria. Shooooosh!

keywords: Lava Room: Hover Boots, 5 Silver Rupees
steps.detail: The Fire Barrier (red) needs the Goron Tunic. Lava Room — most platforms (including the central zig-zag) sink when stood on, so keep the Hover Boots on at all times to stop the center sinking. Jump to the middle, then the right square block for Rupee 1; around to the non-sinking opposing platform, up between the fire sweeps and around the back for Rupee 2; to the far left sinking block with a recovery heart, then the skinny platform, up and Golden-Gauntlets-toss the giant pillar for the Rupee beneath (3); across to the solid platform, kill the Torch Slug (don't grab its Rupee yet), hop to the tossed pillar and — boots off now — jump the sinking block for Rupee 4 and back; grab Rupee 5 by the Torch Slug last. Boots back on, onto the zig-zag platform, Longshot the door target and through.
Grab the jar goodies and Light-Arrow the barrier to unleash the Fire Sage, Darunia.

keywords: Kill 2 Freezards, melt Red Ice, keep Blue Fire
steps.detail: The Water Barrier (blue) is on the lower level. Blue Fire Cavern — mind the stalactites; run forward to spawn two sliding Freezards and kill them as they slide. The door opens: slash the stalagmites, grab a bottle of Blue Fire, melt the Red Ice on the door, and grab MORE Blue Fire before going through (needed ahead). Two chests here — right is a recovery heart, left a frost trap.

keywords: Melt Red Ice, Hammer the switch
steps.detail: Move ice blocks to reach the upper ledge: push the block farthest from the door toward the giant snowball then into the hole; then the block nearest the door to the snowball, to the other, and toward the red-ice ledge. Climb up, melt the Red Ice barrier with Blue Fire, and Hammer the rusty switch — through the door before time runs out.
Slash the jars and Light-Arrow the golden orb: the Sage of Water, Ruto, salutes you and zips you back to the main room.

keywords: Spirit Barrier: Armos Room Silver Rupees
steps.detail: The Spirit Barrier (orange). Armos Room — Bomb the Beamos, Longshot the mid-air Silver Rupee, and pull back the Armos statues to reach the wall Rupees; all collected, through the door. Bombchu Puzzle — kill the two Torch Slugs, hit the crystal switch for a chest of Bombchus (a Spin Attack through the fence on N64), then hit the far switch you can't shoot through the bar slits: align yourself with the hole above the bars and send a Bombchu into the statue.

keywords: Sunlight puzzle: Fire Arrow the cobweb
Mirror Shield the correct Sun Face
steps.detail: Run left into a room of Sun Faces with no sun — look up: a cobweb blocks the light. Fire-Arrow it away (or, without Fire Arrows, pick up flame from the two torches in the previous area with a lined-up arrow). Then Mirror-Shield the light onto the Sun Face just around the corner to the left of the entrance — the others are Wallmaster traps. Through the door.
Collect the jar goodies and Light-Arrow the sphere to unleash the Sage of Spirit, Nabooru.

keywords: Light Barrier: Golden Gauntlets toss the obelisk
Trap Room: Lens the Skulltula + 3 Keese
steps.detail: The final barrier is blocked by a giant silver obelisk — Golden-Gauntlets-toss it aside. Trap Room: Lens out immediately, Hookshot or Bow the central Skulltula, then without moving shoot the three invisible Keese. All dead, a chest gives a Small Key (ignore the other chests — half are ice traps; the safe ones are where the Keese sat).

Lullaby Room — play Zelda's Lullaby on the Triforce for a chest with a Small Key. Rolling Boulder Room — two boulders circle (both go clockwise until you hit one, then they ricochet chaotically, so don't get hit): Rupees 1 and 2 in the left/right holes, 3 and 4 in the center platform's cubbies, and 5 above the center (stand in the middle and Longshot). Through the door.

keywords: Light Barrier: Lens the fake wall
steps.detail: The room looks empty — Lens the fake barrier wall and run through into the true barrier room. Smash the jars and Light-Arrow the sphere to release the Sage of Light, Rauru.
With all six barriers broken, the central pillar's shield dissipates. Stock up on fairies (and confirm your hearts) before heading into the tower. Ganondorf awaits at the top.

missable.deadline: By the final battle — Plenty of Hearts requires having obtained all 20 hearts (10 Heart Containers + 36 Heart Pieces). Cumulative — earned the moment the 20th heart is completed; exact route step is approximate.

keywords: Climb: Dinalfos, Stalfos (Boss Key), Iron Knuckles
steps.detail: Up the tower: run the first hall past the Fire Keese, then the Dinalfos Room (kill the two Dinalfos — they fight simultaneously regardless of targeting). Up the stairs, the Stalfos Boss Key Room: shield-close to make the two Stalfos drop their guard and stab them fast (the second revives if you're slow); the chest holds the Boss Key. Up again, the Iron Knuckle Room: a black and a white one that only wake when struck — take one at a time (the Biggoron's Sword is fast). One more flight to the Boss Door, past a jar-filled antechamber that's part of the boss room (don't smash everything — they hold arrows and magic).

keywords: Deflect the orb, Light Arrow, slash
steps.detail: In the final room Ganondorf plays the organ while Zelda hangs in her crystal; your Triforce pieces resonate and heal you. As a last cruelty he blocks Navi — you can't target for the whole fight, so bring fairies. Run at the start: his ground slam and other attacks knock out the floor blocks (it's like "Don't Break the Ice"), and lingering on the center draws his long-range punch. His main attack is an energy orb (four hearts) — block with the Mirror Shield or, to hurt him, sword-slash it back and forth (like Phantom Ganon) until he's stunned, then Light-Arrow him down to the floor and slash away, retreating to a corner platform (those blocks can't fall) when he recovers. His lightning-ball attack can't be shielded — dodge at the last second, or Light-Arrow / Spin-Attack-reflect it while he charges. Hover Boots help reach the center safely; regular boots allow a jump attack on the way over. Biggoron's Sword speeds it up; if low, drop to the lower room (he won't attack there) for jar refills. About ten good hits and he falls, then vomits (red, or green in later versions) and glows as the tower collapses.

On the roof, Zelda is freed from her crystal; the tower begins to crumble. Follow her down the ramp and through the doors she opens, back through familiar rooms — avoid the falling rocks and keep up. In the Stalfos Room, approach Zelda twice: flames surround her and the two Stalfos reappear (the second revives if you dawdle); beaten, she leaves Recovery Hearts. Outside, jump off the ledge and through her door; in the main room, slip past the ReDead on the bridge and up the final flaming stairs to the exit. The tower sinks into itself in a cool scene.

In the ruins with Zelda, Link and Navi, a loud crash sounds — investigate the pile, and a scene begins: fire erupts and Ganondorf uses the Triforce of Power to become a hideous pot-bellied pig monster, Ganon. He immediately smacks the Master Sword out of your hands and beyond the barrier — you'll fight without it for now.

keywords: Roll between his legs, hit the tail
steps.detail: Ganon's only attack is a dodgeable sword slash; his weak point is the brightly colored tail. Easiest: roll right between his legs to his backside and hit the tail with the Megaton Hammer or Biggoron's Sword, then run before he turns. Alternatively stun him (four ways) and side-hop behind to hit the tail with a Deku Nut, Longshot, Arrow, or melee — Navi's auto-target helpfully picks his face when he faces you and his tail when he's turned. Lure him to hit the nearby ruins for fairies, magic, bombs and arrows if you run low; Nayru's Love helps if you're struggling. After about ten hits he falls and drops the fiery barrier — grab the Master Sword before he rises. He returns a little faster; another ten hits or so and he falls again.

missable.deadline: During this fight — Dark Lord requires defeating Ganon without getting hit, with 3 hearts only

keywords: Final blow with the Master Sword
steps.detail: The final blow must land with the Master Sword — the Biggoron's Sword can weaken but never finish him. Knocked down, Zelda stuns him; quickly equip the Master Sword and slash his head. Ganon stands and slashes once more, then Zelda summons a ball of light and calls the sages, who glow for each element and form a vacuum of light that banishes Ganondorf to the Sacred Realm, vowing to return one day. This is the run-defining moment: the challenge-run achievements (Dark Lord, and the mutually exclusive Blind Explorer / Cartographer) resolve here.

missable.deadline: At the seal — Blind Explorer requires completing the game with no Map or Compass, no skipped medallion, 3 hearts only; Cartographer requires every Map, Compass, and Boss Key. These two are mutually exclusive and cannot both be earned on one playthrough.

keywords: Return the Ocarina, replace the Master Sword
steps.detail: With just Zelda and Link left, she explains she never meant to open the door for Ganondorf or to drag you into this. To set things right, give her back the Ocarina of Time and return the Master Sword to the Pedestal of Time. Then credits roll over scenes of a happy Hyrule — Link leaves the sword in the pedestal, and he and Zelda meet again in the royal garden to warn her before Ganondorf ever enters the Sacred Realm. Time paradoxes — gotta love them. Congratulations, you beat the game!
